    12|# Legal Information
    13|
    14|> This work includes material taken from the System Reference Document 5.1 (“SRD 5.1”) by Wizards of the Coast LLC and available at . The SRD 5.1 is licensed under the Creative Commons Attribution 4.0 International License available at .
    15|
    16|# Races {#chapter-races}
    17|
    18|## Racial Traits {#section-racial-traits}
    19|
    20|The description of each race includes racial traits that are common to members of that race. The following entries appear among the traits of most races.
    21|
    22|### Ability Score Increase
    23|
    24|Every race increases one or more of a character's ability scores.
    25|
    26|### Age
    27|
    28|The age entry notes the age when a member of the race is considered an adult, as well as the race's expected lifespan. This information can help you decide how old your character is at the start of the game. You can choose any age for your character, which could provide an explanation for some of your ability scores. For example, if you play a young or very old character, your age could explain a particularly low Strength or Constitution score, while advanced age could account for a high Intelligence or Wisdom.
    29|
    30|### Alignment
    31|
    32|Most races have tendencies toward certain alignments, described in this entry. These are not binding for player characters, but considering why your dwarf is chaotic, for example, in defiance of lawful dwarf society can help you better define your character.
    33|
    34|### Size
    35|
    36|Characters of most races are Medium, a size category including creatures that are roughly 4 to 8 feet tall. Members of a few races are Small (between 2 and 4 feet tall), which means that certain rules of the game affect them differently. The most important of these rules is that Small characters have trouble wielding heavy weapons, as explained in "Equipment."
    37|
    38|### Speed
    39|
    40|Your speed determines how far you can move when traveling (see "Adventuring") and fighting (see "Combat").
    41|
    42|### Languages
    43|
    44|By virtue of your race, your character can speak, read, and write certain languages.
    45|
    46|### Subraces
    47|
    48|Some races have subraces. Members of a subrace have the traits of the parent race in addition to the traits specified for their subrace. Relationships among subraces vary significantly from race to race and world to world.
    49|
    50|## Dragonborn {#section-dragonborn}
    51|
    52|### Dragonborn Traits
    53|
    54|Your draconic heritage manifests in a variety of traits you share with other dragonborn.
    55|
    56|Ability Score Increase. Your Strength score increases by 2, and your Charisma score increases by 1.
    57|
    58|Age. Young dragonborn grow quickly. They walk hours after hatching, attain the size and development of a 10-year-old human child by the age of 3, and reach adulthood by 15. They live to be around 80.
    59|
    60|Alignment. Dragonborn tend to extremes, making a conscious choice for one side or the other in the cosmic war between good and evil. Most dragonborn are good, but those who side with evil can be terrible villains.
    61|
    62|Size. Dragonborn are taller and heavier than humans, standing well over 6 feet tall and averaging almost 250 pounds. Your size is Medium.
    63|
    64|Speed. Your base walking speed is 30 feet.
    65|
    66|
    67|Draconic Ancestry
    68|
    69|
    70|
    71|
    72|
    73|
    74|
    75|Dragon
    76|Damage Type
    77|Breath Weapon
    78|
    79|
    80|
    81|
    82|Black
    83|Acid
    84|5 by 30 ft. line (Dex. save)
    85|
    86|
    87|Blue
    88|Lightning
    89|5 by 30 ft. line (Dex. save)
    90|
    91|
    92|Brass
    93|Fire
    94|5 by 30 ft. line (Dex. save)
    95|
    96|
    97|Bronze
    98|Lightning
    99|5 by 30 ft. line (Dex. save)
   100|
   101|
   102|Copper
   103|Acid
   104|5 by 30 ft. line (Dex. save)
   105|
   106|
   107|Gold
   108|Fire
   109|15 ft. cone (Dex. save)
   110|
   111|
   112|Green
   113|Poison
   114|15 ft. cone (Con. save)
   115|
   116|
   117|Red
   118|Fire
   119|15 ft. cone (Dex. save)
   120|
   121|
   122|Silver
   123|Cold
   124|15 ft. cone (Con. save)
   125|
   126|
   127|White
   128|Cold
   129|15 ft. cone (Con. save)
   130|
   131|
   132|
   133|
   134|Draconic Ancestry. You have draconic ancestry. Choose one type of dragon from the Draconic Ancestry table. Your breath weapon and damage resistance are determined by the dragon type, as shown in the table.
   135|
   136|Breath Weapon. You can use your action to exhale destructive energy. Your draconic ancestry determines the size, shape, and damage type of the exhalation.
   137|
   138|When you use your breath weapon, each creature in the area of the exhalation must make a saving throw, the type of which is determined by your draconic ancestry. The DC for this saving throw equals 8 + your Constitution modifier + your proficiency bonus. A creature takes 2d6 damage on a failed save, and half as much damage on a successful one. The damage increases to 3d6 at 6th level, 4d6 at 11th level, and 5d6 at 16th level.
   139|
   140|After you use your breath weapon, you can't use it again until you complete a short or long rest.
   141|
   142|Damage Resistance. You have resistance to the damage type associated with your draconic ancestry.
   143|
   144|Languages. You can speak, read, and write Common and Draconic. Draconic is thought to be one of the oldest languages and is often used in the study of magic. The language sounds harsh to most other creatures and includes numerous hard consonants and sibilants.
   145|
   146|## Dwarf {#section-dwarf}
   147|
   148|### Dwarf Traits
   149|
   150|Your dwarf character has an assortment of inborn abilities, part and parcel of dwarven nature.
   151|
   152|Ability Score Increase. Your Constitution score increases by 2.
   153|
   154|Age. Dwarves mature at the same rate as humans, but they're considered young until they reach the age of 50. On average, they live about 350 years.
   155|
   156|Alignment. Most dwarves are lawful, believing firmly in the benefits of a well-ordered society. They tend toward good as well, with a strong sense of fair play and a belief that everyone deserves to share in the benefits of a just order.
   157|
   158|Size. Dwarves stand between 4 and 5 feet tall and average about 150 pounds. Your size is Medium.
   159|
   160|Speed. Your base walking speed is 25 feet. Your speed is not reduced by wearing heavy armor.
   161|
   162|Darkvision. Accustomed to life underground, you have superior vision in dark and dim conditions. You can see in dim light within 60 feet of you as if it were bright light, and in darkness as if it were dim light. You can't discern color in darkness, only shades of gray.
   163|
   164|Dwarven Resilience. You have advantage on saving throws against poison, and you have resistance against poison damage.
   165|
   166|Dwarven Combat Training. You have proficiency with the battleaxe, handaxe, light hammer, and warhammer.
   167|
   168|Tool Proficiency. You gain proficiency with the artisan's tools of your choice: smith's tools, brewer's supplies, or mason's tools.
   169|
   170|Stonecunning. Whenever you make an Intelligence (History) check related to the origin of stonework, you are considered proficient in the History skill and add double your proficiency bonus to the check, instead of your normal proficiency bonus.
   171|
   172|Languages. You can speak, read, and write Common and Dwarvish. Dwarvish is full of hard consonants and guttural sounds, and those characteristics spill over into whatever other language a dwarf might speak.
   173|
   174|#### Hill Dwarf
   175|
   176|As a hill dwarf, you have keen senses, deep intuition, and remarkable resilience.
   177|
   178|Ability Score Increase. Your Wisdom score increases by 1.
   179|
   180|Dwarven Toughness. Your hit point maximum increases by 1, and it increases by 1 every time you gain a level.
   181|
   182|## Elf {#section-elf}
   183|
   184|### Elf Traits
   185|
   186|Your elf character has a variety of natural abilities, the result of thousands of years of elven refinement.
   187|
   188|Ability Score Increase. Your Dexterity score increases by 2.
   189|
   190|Age. Although elves reach physical maturity at about the same age as humans, the elven understanding of adulthood goes beyond physical growth to encompass worldly experience. An elf typically claims adulthood and an adult name around the age of 100 and can live to be 750 years old.
   191|
   192|Alignment. Elves love freedom, variety, and self-expression, so they lean strongly toward the gentler aspects of chaos. They value and protect others' freedom as well as their own, and they are more often good than not.
   193|
   194|Size. Elves range from under 5 to over 6 feet tall and have slender builds. Your size is Medium.
   195|
   196|Speed. Your base walking speed is 30 feet.
   197|
   198|Darkvision. Accustomed to twilit forests and the night sky, you have superior vision in dark and dim conditions. You can see in dim light within 60 feet of you as if it were bright light, and in darkness as if it were dim light. You can't discern color in darkness, only shades of gray.
   199|
   200|Keen Senses. You have proficiency in the Perception skill.
   201|
   202|Fey Ancestry. You have advantage on saving throws against being charmed, and magic can't put you to sleep.
   203|
   204|Trance. Elves don't need to sleep. Instead, they meditate deeply, remaining semiconscious, for 4 hours a day. (The Common word for such meditation is "trance.") While meditating, you can dream after a fashion; such dreams are actually mental exercises that have become reflexive through years of practice. After resting in this way, you gain the same benefit that a human does from 8 hours of sleep.
   205|
   206|Languages. You can speak, read, and write Common and Elvish. Elvish is fluid, with subtle intonations and intricate grammar. Elven literature is rich and varied, and their songs and poems are famous among other races. Many bards learn their language so they can add Elvish ballads to their repertoires.
   207|
   208|#### High Elf
   209|
   210|As a high elf, you have a keen mind and a mastery of at least the basics of magic. In many fantasy gaming worlds, there are two kinds of high elves. One type is haughty and reclusive, believing themselves to be superior to non-elves and even other elves. The other type is more common and more friendly, and often encountered among humans and other races.
   211|
   212|Ability Score Increase. Your Intelligence score increases by 1.
   213|
   214|Elf Weapon Training. You have proficiency with the longsword, shortsword, shortbow, and longbow.
   215|
   216|Cantrip. You know one cantrip of your choice from the wizard spell list. Intelligence is your spellcasting ability for it.
   217|
   218|Extra Language. You can speak, read, and write one extra language of your choice.
   219|
   220|## Gnome {#section-gnome}
   221|
   222|### Gnome Traits
   223|
   224|Your gnome character has certain characteristics in common with all other gnomes.
   225|
   226|Ability Score Increase. Your Intelligence score increases by 2.
   227|
   228|Age. Gnomes mature at the same rate humans do, and most are expected to settle down into an adult life by around age 40. They can live 350 to almost 500 years.
   229|
   230|Alignment. Gnomes are most often good. Those who tend toward law are sages, engineers, researchers, scholars, investigators, or inventors. Those who tend toward chaos are minstrels, tricksters, wanderers, or fanciful jewelers. Gnomes are good-hearted, and even the tricksters among them are more playful than vicious.
   231|
   232|Size. Gnomes are between 3 and 4 feet tall and average about 40 pounds. Your size is Small.
   233|
   234|Speed. Your base walking speed is 25 feet.
   235|
   236|Darkvision. Accustomed to life underground, you have superior vision in dark and dim conditions. You can see in dim light within 60 feet of you as if it were bright light, and in darkness as if it were dim light. You can't discern color in darkness, only shades of gray.
   237|
   238|Gnome Cunning. You have advantage on all Intelligence, Wisdom, and Charisma saving throws against magic.
   239|
   240|Languages. You can speak, read, and write Common and Gnomish. The Gnomish language, which uses the Dwarvish script, is renowned for its technical treatises and its catalogs of knowledge about the natural world.
   241|
   242|#### Rock Gnome
   243|
   244|As a rock gnome, you have a natural inventiveness and hardiness beyond that of other gnomes.
   245|
   246|Ability Score Increase. Your Constitution score increases by 1.
   247|
   248|Artificer's Lore. Whenever you make an Intelligence (History) check related to magic items, alchemical objects, or technological devices, you can add twice your proficiency bonus, instead of any proficiency bonus you normally apply.
   249|
   250|Tinker. You have proficiency with artisan's tools (tinker's tools). Using those tools, you can spend 1 hour and 10 gp worth of materials to construct a Tiny clockwork device (AC 5, 1 hp). The device ceases to function after 24 hours (unless you spend 1 hour repairing it to keep the device functioning), or when you use your action to dismantle it; at that time, you can reclaim the materials used to create it. You can have up to three such devices active at a time.
   251|
   252|When you create a device, choose one of the following options:
   253|
   254|Clockwork Toy. This toy is a clockwork animal, monster, or person, such as a frog, mouse, bird, dragon, or soldier. When placed on the ground, the toy moves 5 feet across the ground on each of your turns in a random direction. It makes noises as appropriate to the creature it represents.
   255|
   256|Fire Starter. The device produces a miniature flame, which you can use to light a candle, torch, or campfire. Using the device requires your action.
   257|
   258|Music Box. When opened, this music box plays a single song at a moderate volume. The box stops playing when it reaches the song's end or when it is closed.
   259|
   260|## Half-Elf {#section-half-elf}
   261|
   262|### Half-Elf Traits
   263|
   264|Your half-elf character has some qualities in common with elves and some that are unique to half-elves.
   265|
   266|Ability Score Increase. Your Charisma score increases by 2, and two other ability scores of your choice increase by 1.
   267|
   268|Age. Half-elves mature at the same rate humans do and reach adulthood around the age of 20. They live much longer than humans, however, often exceeding 180 years.
   269|
   270|Alignment. Half-elves share the chaotic bent of their elven heritage. They value both personal freedom and creative expression, demonstrating neither love of leaders nor desire for followers. They chafe at rules, resent others' demands, and sometimes prove unreliable, or at least unpredictable.
   271|
   272|Size. Half-elves are about the same size as humans, ranging from 5 to 6 feet tall. Your size is Medium.
   273|
   274|Speed. Your base walking speed is 30 feet.
   275|
   276|Darkvision. Thanks to your elf blood, you have superior vision in dark and dim conditions. You can see in dim light within 60 feet of you as if it were bright light, and in darkness as if it were dim light. You can't discern color in darkness, only shades of gray.
   277|
   278|Fey Ancestry. You have advantage on saving throws against being charmed, and magic can't put you to sleep.
   279|
   280|Skill Versatility. You gain proficiency in two skills of your choice.
   281|
   282|Languages. You can speak, read, and write Common, Elvish, and one extra language of your choice.
   283|
   284|## Half-Orc {#section-half-orc}
   285|
   286|### Half-Orc Traits
   287|
   288|Your half-orc character has certain traits deriving from your orc ancestry.
   289|
   290|Ability Score Increase. Your Strength score increases by 2, and your Constitution score increases by 1.
   291|
   292|Age. Half-orcs mature a little faster than humans, reaching adulthood around age 14. They age noticeably faster and rarely live longer than 75 years.
   293|
   294|Alignment. Half-orcs inherit a tendency toward chaos from their orc parents and are not strongly inclined toward good. Half-orcs raised among orcs and willing to live out their lives among them are usually evil.
   295|
   296|Size. Half-orcs are somewhat larger and bulkier than humans, and they range from 5 to well over 6 feet tall. Your size is Medium.
   297|
   298|Speed. Your base walking speed is 30 feet.
   299|
   300|Darkvision. Thanks to your orc blood, you have superior vision in dark and dim conditions. You can see in dim light within 60 feet of you as if it were bright light, and in darkness as if it were dim light. You can't discern color in darkness, only shades of gray.
   301|
   302|Menacing. You gain proficiency in the Intimidation skill.
   303|
   304|Relentless Endurance. When you are reduced to 0 hit points but not killed outright, you can drop to 1 hit point instead. You can't use this feature again until you finish a long rest.
   305|
   306|Savage Attacks. When you score a critical hit with a melee weapon attack, you can roll one of the weapon's damage dice one additional time and add it to the extra damage of the critical hit.
   307|
   308|Languages. You can speak, read, and write Common and Orc. Orc is a harsh, grating language with hard consonants. It has no script of its own but is written in the Dwarvish script.
   309|
   310|## Halfling {#section-halfling}
   311|
   312|### Halfling Traits
   313|
   314|Your halfling character has a number of traits in common with all other halflings.
   315|
   316|Ability Score Increase. Your Dexterity score increases by 2.
   317|
   318|Age. A halfling reaches adulthood at the age of 20 and generally lives into the middle of his or her second century.
   319|
   320|Alignment. Most halflings are lawful good. As a rule, they are good-hearted and kind, hate to see others in pain, and have no tolerance for oppression. They are also very orderly and traditional, leaning heavily on the support of their community and the comfort of their old ways.
   321|
   322|Size. Halflings average about 3 feet tall and weigh about 40 pounds. Your size is Small.
   323|
   324|Speed. Your base walking speed is 25 feet.
   325|
   326|Lucky. When you roll a 1 on the d20 for an attack roll, ability check, or saving throw, you can reroll the die and must use the new roll.
   327|
   328|Brave. You have advantage on saving throws against being frightened.
   329|
   330|Halfling Nimbleness. You can move through the space of any creature that is of a size larger than yours.
   331|
   332|Languages. You can speak, read, and write Common and Halfling. The Halfling language isn't secret, but halflings are loath to share it with others. They write very little, so they don't have a rich body of literature. Their oral tradition, however, is very strong. Almost all halflings speak Common to converse with the people in whose lands they dwell or through which they are traveling.
   333|
   334|#### Lightfoot
   335|
   336|As a lightfoot halfling, you can easily hide from notice, even using other people as cover. You're inclined to be affable and get along well with others.
   337|
   338|Lightfoots are more prone to wanderlust than other halflings, and often dwell alongside other races or take up a nomadic life.
   339|
   340|Ability Score Increase. Your Charisma score increases by 1.
   341|
   342|Naturally Stealthy. You can attempt to hide even when you are obscured only by a creature that is at least one size larger than you.
   343|
   344|## Human {#section-human}
   345|
   346|### Human Traits
   347|
   348|It's hard to make generalizations about humans, but your human character has these traits.
   349|
   350|Ability Score Increase. Your ability scores each increase by 1.
   351|
   352|Age. Humans reach adulthood in their late teens and live less than a century.
   353|
   354|Alignment. Humans tend toward no particular alignment. The best and the worst are found among them.
   355|
   356|Size. Humans vary widely in height and build, from barely 5 feet to well over 6 feet tall. Regardless of your position in that range, your size is Medium.
   357|
   358|Speed. Your base walking speed is 30 feet.
   359|
   360|Languages. You can speak, read, and write Common and one extra language of your choice. Humans typically learn the languages of other peoples they deal with, including obscure dialects. They are fond of sprinkling their speech with words borrowed from other tongues: Orc curses, Elvish musical expressions, Dwarvish military phrases, and so on.
   361|
   362|## Tiefling {#section-tiefling}
   363|
   364|### Tiefling Traits
   365|
   366|Tieflings share certain racial traits as a result of their infernal descent.
   367|
   368|Ability Score Increase. Your Intelligence score increases by 1, and your Charisma score increases by 2.
   369|
   370|Age. Tieflings mature at the same rate as humans but live a few years longer.
   371|
   372|Alignment. Tieflings might not have an innate tendency toward evil, but many of them end up there. Evil or not, an independent nature inclines many tieflings toward a chaotic alignment.
   373|
   374|Size. Tieflings are about the same size and build as humans. Your size is Medium.
   375|
   376|Speed. Your base walking speed is 30 feet.
   377|
   378|Darkvision. Thanks to your infernal heritage, you have superior vision in dark and dim conditions. You can see in dim light within 60 feet of you as if it were bright light, and in darkness as if it were dim light. You can't discern color in darkness, only shades of gray.
   379|
   380|Hellish Resistance. You have resistance to fire damage.
   381|
   382|Infernal Legacy. You know the thaumaturgy cantrip. When you reach 3rd level, you can cast the hellish rebuke spell as a 2nd-level spell once with this trait and regain the ability to do so when you finish a long rest. When you reach 5th level, you can cast the darkness spell once with this trait and regain the ability to do so when you finish a long rest. Charisma is your spellcasting ability for these spells.
   383|
   384|Languages. You can speak, read, and write Common and Infernal.
   385|
   386|# Classes {#chapter-classes}
   387|
   388|## Barbarian {#section-barbarian}
   389|
   390|### Class Features
   391|
   392|
   393|The Barbarian

   552|
   553|
   554|As a barbarian, you gain the following class features.
   555|
   556|#### Hit Points
   557|
   558|Hit Dice: 1d12 per barbarian level
   559|
   560|Hit Points at 1st Level: 12 + your Constitution modifier
   561|
   562|Hit Points at Higher Levels: 1d12 (or 7) + your Constitution modifier per barbarian level after 1st
   563|
   564|#### Proficiencies
   565|
   566|Armor: Light armor, medium armor, shields
   567|
   568|Weapons: Simple weapons, martial weapons
   569|
   570|Tools: None
   571|
   572|Saving Throws: Strength, Constitution
   573|
   574|Skills: Choose two from Animal Handling, Athletics, Intimidation, Nature, Perception, and Survival
   575|
   576|#### Equipment
   577|
   578|You start with the following equipment, in addition to the equipment granted by your background:
   579|
   580|-   (a) a greataxe or (b) any martial melee weapon
   581|-   (a) two handaxes or (b) any simple weapon
   582|-   An explorer's pack and four javelins
   583|
   584|### Rage
   585|
   586|In battle, you fight with primal ferocity. On your turn, you can enter a rage as a bonus action.
   587|
   588|While raging, you gain the following benefits if you aren't wearing heavy armor:
   589|
   590|-   You have advantage on Strength checks and Strength saving throws.
   591|-   When you make a melee weapon attack using Strength, you gain a bonus to the damage roll that increases as you gain levels as a barbarian, as shown in the Rage Damage column of the Barbarian table.
   592|-   You have resistance to bludgeoning, piercing, and slashing damage.
   593|
   594|If you are able to cast spells, you can't cast them or concentrate on them while raging.
   595|
   596|Your rage lasts for 1 minute. It ends early if you are knocked unconscious or if your turn ends and you haven't attacked a hostile creature since your last turn or taken damage since then. You can also end your rage on your turn as a bonus action.
   597|
   598|Once you have raged the number of times shown for your barbarian level in the Rages column of the Barbarian table, you must finish a long rest before you can rage again.
   599|
   600|### Unarmored Defense
   601|
   602|While you are not wearing any armor, your Armor Class equals 10 + your Dexterity modifier + your Constitution modifier. You can use a shield and still gain this benefit.
   603|
   604|### Reckless Attack
   605|
   606|Starting at 2nd level, you can throw aside all concern for defense to attack with fierce desperation. When you make your first attack on your turn, you can decide to attack recklessly. Doing so gives you advantage on melee weapon attack rolls using Strength during this turn, but attack rolls against you have advantage until your next turn.
   607|
   608|### Danger Sense
   609|
   610|At 2nd level, you gain an uncanny sense of when things nearby aren't as they should be, giving you an edge when you dodge away from danger.
   611|
   612|You have advantage on Dexterity saving throws against effects that you can see, such as traps and spells. To gain this benefit, you can't be blinded, deafened, or incapacitated.
   613|
   614|### Primal Path
   615|
   616|At 3rd level, you choose a path (see "Barbarian Paths") that shapes the nature of your rage. Your choice grants you features at 3rd level and again at 6th, 10th, and 14th levels.
   617|
   618|### Ability Score Improvement
   619|
   620|When you reach 4th level, and again at 8th, 12th, 16th, and 19th level, you can increase one ability score of your choice by 2, or you can increase two ability scores of your choice by 1. As normal, you can't increase an ability score above 20 using this feature.
   621|
   622|### Extra Attack
   623|
   624|Beginning at 5th level, you can attack twice, instead of once, whenever you take the Attack action on your turn.
   625|
   626|### Fast Movement
   627|
   628|Starting at 5th level, your speed increases by 10 feet while you aren't wearing heavy armor.
   629|
   630|### Feral Instinct
   631|
   632|By 7th level, your instincts are so honed that you have advantage on initiative rolls.
   633|
   634|Additionally, if you are surprised at the beginning of combat and aren't incapacitated, you can act normally on your first turn, but only if you enter your rage before doing anything else on that turn.
   635|
   636|### Brutal Critical
   637|
   638|Beginning at 9th level, you can roll one additional weapon damage die when determining the extra damage for a critical hit with a melee attack.
   639|
   640|This increases to two additional dice at 13th level and three additional dice at 17th level.
   641|
   642|### Relentless Rage
   643|
   644|Starting at 11th level, your rage can keep you fighting despite grievous wounds. If you drop to 0 hit points while you're raging and don't die outright, you can make a DC 10 Constitution saving throw. If you succeed, you drop to 1 hit point instead.
   645|
   646|Each time you use this feature after the first, the DC increases by 5. When you finish a short or long rest, the DC resets to 10.
   647|
   648|### Persistent Rage
   649|
   650|Beginning at 15th level, your rage is so fierce that it ends early only if you fall unconscious or if you choose to end it.
   651|
   652|### Indomitable Might
   653|
   654|Beginning at 18th level, if your total for a Strength check is less than your Strength score, you can use that score in place of the total.
   655|
   656|### Primal Champion
   657|
   658|At 20th level, you embody the power of the wilds. Your Strength and Constitution scores increase by 4. Your maximum for those scores is now 24.
   659|
   660|## Barbarian Paths {#section-barbarian-paths}
   661|
   662|### Path of the Berserker
   663|
   664|For some barbarians, rage is a means to an end—that end being violence. The Path of the Berserker is a path of untrammeled fury, slick with blood. As you enter the berserker's rage, you thrill in the chaos of battle, heedless of your own health or well-being.
   665|
   666|#### Frenzy
   667|
   668|Starting when you choose this path at 3rd level, you can go into a frenzy when you rage. If you do so, for the duration of your rage you can make a single melee weapon attack as a bonus action on each of your turns after this one. When your rage ends, you suffer one level of exhaustion.
   669|
   670|#### Mindless Rage
   671|
   672|Beginning at 6th level, you can't be charmed or frightened while raging. If you are charmed or frightened when you enter your rage, the effect is suspended for the duration of the rage.
   673|
   674|#### Intimidating Presence
   675|
   676|Beginning at 10th level, you can use your action to frighten someone with your menacing presence. When you do so, choose one creature that you can see within 30 feet of you. If the creature can see or hear you, it must succeed on a Wisdom saving throw (DC equal to 8 + your proficiency bonus + your Charisma modifier) or be frightened of you until the end of your next turn. On subsequent turns, you can use your action to extend the duration of this effect on the frightened creature until the end of your next turn. This effect ends if the creature ends its turn out of line of sight or more than 60 feet away from you.
   677|
   678|If the creature succeeds on its saving throw, you can't use this feature on that creature again for 24 hours.
   679|
   680|#### Retaliation
   681|
   682|Starting at 14th level, when you take damage from a creature that is within 5 feet of you, you can use your reaction to make a melee weapon attack against that creature.
   683|

   846|
   847|
   848|
   849|
   850|As a bard, you gain the following class features.
   851|
   852|#### Hit Points
   853|
   854|Hit Dice: 1d8 per bard level
   855|
   856|Hit Points at 1st Level: 8 + your Constitution modifier
   857|
   858|Hit Points at Higher Levels: 1d8 (or 5) + your Constitution modifier per bard level after 1st
   859|
   860|#### Proficiencies
   861|
   862|Armor: Light armor
   863|
   864|Weapons: Simple weapons, hand crossbows, longswords, rapiers, shortswords
   865|
   866|Tools: Three musical instruments of your choice
   867|
   868|Saving Throws: Dexterity, Charisma
   869|
   870|Skills: Choose any three
   871|
   872|#### Equipment
   873|
   874|You start with the following equipment, in addition to the equipment granted by your background:
   875|
   876|-   (a) a rapier, (b) a longsword, or (c) any simple weapon
   877|-   (a) a diplomat's pack or (b) an entertainer's pack
   878|-   (a) a lute or (b) any other musical instrument
   879|-   Leather armor and a dagger
   880|
   881|### Spellcasting
   882|
   883|You have learned to untangle and reshape the fabric of reality in harmony with your wishes and music. Your spells are part of your vast repertoire, magic that you can tune to different situations.
   884|
   885|#### Cantrips
   886|
   887|You know two cantrips of your choice from the bard spell list. You learn additional bard cantrips of your choice at higher levels, as shown in the Cantrips Known column of the Bard table.
   888|
   889|#### Spell Slots
   890|
   891|
   892|Bard Spell Slots per Level
   893|
   894|
   895|
   896|
   897|

  1159|2
  1160|1
  1161|1
  1162|
  1163|
  1164|
  1165|
  1166|The Bard Spell Slots per Level table shows how many spell slots you have to cast your spells of 1st level and higher. To cast one of these spells, you must expend a slot of the spell's level or higher. You regain all expended spell slots when you finish a long rest.
  1167|
  1168|For example, if you know the 1st-level spell cure wounds and have a 1st-level and a 2nd-level spell slot available, you can cast cure wounds using either slot.
  1169|
  1170|#### Spells Known of 1st Level and Higher
  1171|
  1172|You know four 1st-level spells of your choice from the bard spell list.
  1173|
  1174|The Spells Known column of the Bard table shows when you learn more bard spells of your choice. Each of these spells must be of a level for which you have spell slots, as shown on the table. For instance, when you reach 3rd level in this class, you can learn one new spell of 1st or 2nd level.
  1175|
  1176|Additionally, when you gain a level in this class, you can choose one of the bard spells you know and replace it with another spell from the bard spell list, which also must be of a level for which you have spell slots.
  1177|
  1178|#### Spellcasting Ability
  1179|
  1180|Charisma is your spellcasting ability for your bard spells. Your magic comes from the heart and soul you pour into the performance of your music or oration. You use your Charisma whenever a spell refers to your spellcasting ability. In addition, you use your Charisma modifier when setting the saving throw DC for a bard spell you cast and when making an attack roll with one.
  1181|
  1182|
  1183|
  1184|
  1185|
  1186|
  1187|
  1188|Spell save DC = 8 + your proficiency bonus + your Charisma modifier
  1189|
  1190|
  1191|Spell attack modifier = your proficiency bonus + your Charisma modifier
  1192|
  1193|
  1194|
  1195|
  1196|#### Ritual Casting
  1197|
  1198|You can cast any bard spell you know as a ritual if that spell has the ritual tag.
  1199|
  1200|#### Spellcasting Focus
  1201|
  1202|You can use a musical instrument (see "Equipment") as a spellcasting focus for your bard spells.
  1203|
  1204|### Bardic Inspiration
  1205|
  1206|You can inspire others through stirring words or music. To do so, you use a bonus action on your turn to choose one creature other than yourself within 60 feet of you who can hear you. That creature gains one Bardic Inspiration die, a d6.
  1207|
  1208|Once within the next 10 minutes, the creature can roll the die and add the number rolled to one ability check, attack roll, or saving throw it makes. The creature can wait until after it rolls the d20 before deciding to use the Bardic Inspiration die, but must decide before the GM says whether the roll succeeds or fails. Once the Bardic Inspiration die is rolled, it is lost. A creature can have only one Bardic Inspiration die at a time.
  1209|
  1210|You can use this feature a number of times equal to your Charisma modifier (a minimum of once). You regain any expended uses when you finish a long rest.
  1211|
  1212|Your Bardic Inspiration die changes when you reach certain levels in this class. The die becomes a d8 at 5th level, a d10 at 10th level, and a d12 at 15th level.
  1213|
  1214|### Jack of All Trades
  1215|
  1216|Starting at 2nd level, you can add half your proficiency bonus, rounded down, to any ability check you make that doesn't already include your proficiency bonus.
  1217|
  1218|### Song of Rest
  1219|
  1220|Beginning at 2nd level, you can use soothing music or oration to help revitalize your wounded allies during a short rest. If you or any friendly creatures who can hear your performance regain hit points at the end of the short rest by spending one or more Hit Dice, each of those creatures regains an extra 1d6 hit points.
  1221|
  1222|The extra hit points increase when you reach certain levels in this class: to 1d8 at 9th level, to 1d10 at 13th level, and to 1d12 at 17th level.
  1223|
  1224|### Bard College
  1225|
  1226|At 3rd level, you delve into the advanced techniques of a bard college (see "Bard Colleges") of your choice. Your choice grants you features at 3rd level and again at 6th and 14th level.
  1227|
  1228|### Expertise
  1229|
  1230|At 3rd level, choose two of your skill proficiencies. Your proficiency bonus is doubled for any ability check you make that uses either of the chosen proficiencies.
  1231|
  1232|At 10th level, you can choose another two skill proficiencies to gain this benefit.
  1233|
  1234|### Ability Score Improvement
  1235|
  1236|When you reach 4th level, and again at 8th, 12th, 16th, and 19th level, you can increase one ability score of your choice by 2, or you can increase two ability scores of your choice by 1. As normal, you can't increase an ability score above 20 using this feature.
  1237|
  1238|### Font of Inspiration
  1239|
  1240|Beginning when you reach 5th level, you regain all of your expended uses of Bardic Inspiration when you finish a short or long rest.
  1241|
  1242|### Countercharm
  1243|
  1244|At 6th level, you gain the ability to use musical notes or words of power to disrupt mind-influencing effects. As an action, you can start a performance that lasts until the end of your next turn. During that time, you and any friendly creatures within 30 feet of you have advantage on saving throws against being frightened or charmed. A creature must be able to hear you to gain this benefit. The performance ends early if you are incapacitated or silenced or if you voluntarily end it (no action required).
  1245|
  1246|### Magical Secrets
  1247|
  1248|By 10th level, you have plundered magical knowledge from a wide spectrum of disciplines. Choose two spells from any class, including this one. A spell you choose must be of a level you can cast, as shown on the Bard table, or a cantrip.
  1249|
  1250|The chosen spells count as bard spells for you and are included in the number in the Spells Known column of the Bard table.
  1251|
  1252|You learn two additional spells from any class at 14th level and again at 18th level.
  1253|
  1254|### Superior Inspiration
  1255|
  1256|At 20th level, when you roll initiative and have no uses of Bardic Inspiration left, you regain one use.
  1257|
  1258|## Bard Colleges {#section-bard-colleges}
  1259|
  1260|### College of Lore
  1261|
  1262|Bards of the College of Lore know something about most things, collecting bits of knowledge from sources as diverse as scholarly tomes and peasant tales. Whether singing folk ballads in taverns or elaborate compositions in royal courts, these bards use their gifts to hold audiences spellbound. When the applause dies down, the audience members might find themselves questioning everything they held to be true, from their faith in the priesthood of the local temple to their loyalty to the king.
  1263|
  1264|The loyalty of these bards lies in the pursuit of beauty and truth, not in fealty to a monarch or following the tenets of a deity. A noble who keeps such a bard as a herald or advisor knows that the bard would rather be honest than politic.
  1265|
  1266|The college's members gather in libraries and sometimes in actual colleges, complete with classrooms and dormitories, to share their lore with one another. They also meet at festivals or affairs of state, where they can expose corruption, unravel lies, and poke fun at self-important figures of authority.
  1267|
  1268|#### Bonus Proficiencies
  1269|
  1270|When you join the College of Lore at 3rd level, you gain proficiency with three skills of your choice.
  1271|
  1272|#### Cutting Words
  1273|
  1274|Also at 3rd level, you learn how to use your wit to distract, confuse, and otherwise sap the confidence and competence of others. When a creature that you can see within 60 feet of you makes an attack roll, an ability check, or a damage roll, you can use your reaction to expend one of your uses of Bardic Inspiration, rolling a Bardic Inspiration die and subtracting the number rolled from the creature's roll. You can choose to use this feature after the creature makes its roll, but before the GM determines whether the attack roll or ability check succeeds or fails, or before the creature deals its damage. The creature is immune if it can't hear you or if it's immune to being charmed.
  1275|
  1276|#### Additional Magical Secrets
  1277|
  1278|At 6th level, you learn two spells of your choice from any class. A spell you choose must be of a level you can cast, as shown on the Bard table, or a cantrip. The chosen spells count as bard spells for you but don't count against the number of bard spells you know.
  1279|
  1280|#### Peerless Skill
  1281|
  1282|Starting at 14th level, when you make an ability check, you can expend one use of Bardic Inspiration. Roll a Bardic Inspiration die and add the number rolled to your ability check. You can choose to do so after you roll the die for the ability check, but before the GM tells you whether you succeed or fail.
  1283|
  1284|## Cleric {#section-cleric}
  1285|
  1286|### Class Features
  1287|
  1288|
  1289|The Cleric
  1290|
  1291|
  1292|
  1293|
  1294|
  1295|
  1296|
  1297|
  1298|Level
  1299|Proficiency Bonus
  1300|Features
  1301|Cantrips Known
  1302|
  1303|
  1304|
  1305|
  1306|1st
  1307|+2
  1308|Spellcasting, Divine Domain
  1309|3
  1310|
  1311|
  1312|2nd
  1313|+2
  1314|Channel Divinity (1/rest), Divine Domain feature
  1315|3
  1316|
  1317|
  1318|3rd
  1319|+2
  1320|—
  1321|3
  1322|
  1323|
  1324|4th
  1325|+2
  1326|Ability Score Improvement
  1327|4
  1328|

  1427|
  1428|As a cleric, you gain the following class features.
  1429|
  1430|#### Hit Points
  1431|
  1432|Hit Dice: 1d8 per cleric level
  1433|
  1434|Hit Points at 1st Level: 8 + your Constitution modifier
  1435|
  1436|Hit Points at Higher Levels: 1d8 (or 5) + your Constitution modifier per cleric level after 1st
  1437|
  1438|#### Proficiencies
  1439|
  1440|Armor: Light armor, medium armor, shields
  1441|
  1442|Weapons: Simple weapons
  1443|
  1444|Tools: None
  1445|
  1446|Saving Throws: Wisdom, Charisma
  1447|
  1448|Skills: Choose two from History, Insight, Medicine, Persuasion, and Religion
  1449|
  1450|### Equipment
  1451|
  1452|You start with the following equipment, in addition to the equipment granted by your background:
  1453|
  1454|-   (a) a mace or (b) a warhammer (if proficient)
  1455|-   (a) scale mail, (b) leather armor, or (c) chain mail (if proficient)
  1456|-   (a) a light crossbow and 20 bolts or (b) any simple weapon
  1457|-   (a) a priest's pack or (b) an explorer's pack
  1458|-   A shield and a holy symbol
  1459|
  1460|### Spellcasting
  1461|
  1462|As a conduit for divine power, you can cast cleric spells.
  1463|
  1464|#### Cantrips
  1465|
  1466|At 1st level, you know three cantrips of your choice from the cleric spell list. You learn additional cleric cantrips of your choice at higher levels, as shown in the Cantrips Known column of the Cleric table.
  1467|
  1468|#### Preparing and Casting Spells
  1469|
  1470|

  1707|18th
  1708|4
  1709|3
  1710|3
  1711|3
  1712|3

  1714|1
  1715|1

  1717|
  1718|
  1719|19th
  1720|4
  1721|3
  1722|3
  1723|3
  1724|3
  1725|2
  1726|1
  1727|1
  1728|1
  1729|
  1730|
  1731|20th
  1732|4
  1733|3
  1734|3
  1735|3
  1736|3
  1737|2
  1738|2
  1739|1
  1740|1
  1741|
  1742|
  1743|
  1744|
  1745|The Cleric Spell Slots per Level table shows how many spell slots you have to cast your spells of 1st level and higher. To cast one of these spells, you must expend a slot of the spell's level or higher. You regain all expended spell slots when you finish a long rest.
  1746|
  1747|You prepare the list of cleric spells that are available for you to cast, choosing from the cleric spell list. When you do so, choose a number of cleric spells equal to your Wisdom modifier + your cleric level (minimum of one spell). The spells must be of a level for which you have spell slots.
  1748|
  1749|For example, if you are a 3rd-level cleric, you have four 1st-level and two 2nd-level spell slots. With a Wisdom of 16, your list of prepared spells can include six spells of 1st or 2nd level, in any combination. If you prepare the 1st-level spell cure wounds, you can cast it using a 1st-level or 2nd-level slot. Casting the spell doesn't remove it from your list of prepared spells.
  1750|
  1751|You can change your list of prepared spells when you finish a long rest. Preparing a new list of cleric spells requires time spent in prayer and meditation: at least 1 minute per spell level for each spell on your list.
  1752|
  1753|#### Spellcasting Ability
  1754|
  1755|Wisdom is your spellcasting ability for your cleric spells. The power of your spells comes from your devotion to your deity. You use your Wisdom whenever a cleric spell refers to your spellcasting ability. In addition, you use your Wisdom modifier when setting the saving throw DC for a cleric spell you cast and when making an attack roll with one.
  1756|
  1757|
  1758|
  1759|
  1760|
  1761|
  1762|
  1763|Spell save DC = 8 + your proficiency bonus + your Wisdom modifier
  1764|
  1765|
  1766|Spell attack modifier = your proficiency bonus + your Wisdom modifier
  1767|
  1768|
  1769|
  1770|
  1771|#### Ritual Casting
  1772|
  1773|You can cast a cleric spell as a ritual if that spell has the ritual tag and you have the spell prepared.
  1774|
  1775|#### Spellcasting Focus
  1776|
  1777|You can use a holy symbol (see "Equipment") as a spellcasting focus for your cleric spells.
  1778|
  1779|### Divine Domain
  1780|
  1781|Choose one domain (see "Domains") related to your deity. Each domain provides examples of gods associated with it. Your choice grants you domain spells and other features when you choose it at 1st level. It also grants you additional ways to use Channel Divinity when you gain that feature at 2nd level, and additional benefits at 6th, 8th, and 17th levels.
  1782|
  1783|#### Domain Spells
  1784|
  1785|Each domain has a list of spells—its domain spells—that you gain at the cleric levels noted in the domain description. Once you gain a domain spell, you always have it prepared, and it doesn't count against the number of spells you can prepare each day.
  1786|
  1787|If you have a domain spell that doesn't appear on the cleric spell list, the spell is nonetheless a cleric spell for you.
  1788|
  1789|### Channel Divinity
  1790|
  1791|At 2nd level, you gain the ability to channel divine energy directly from your deity, using that energy to fuel magical effects. You start with two such effects: Turn Undead and an effect determined by your domain. Some domains grant you additional effects as you advance in levels, as noted in the domain description.
  1792|
  1793|When you use your Channel Divinity, you choose which effect to create. You must then finish a short or long rest to use your Channel Divinity again.
  1794|
  1795|Some Channel Divinity effects require saving throws. When you use such an effect from this class, the DC equals your cleric spell save DC.
  1796|
  1797|Beginning at 6th level, you can use your Channel Divinity twice between rests, and beginning at 18th level, you can use it three times between rests. When you finish a short or long rest, you regain your expended uses.
  1798|
  1799|#### Channel Divinity: Turn Undead
  1800|
  1801|As an action, you present your holy symbol and speak a prayer censuring the undead. Each undead that can see or hear you within 30 feet of you must make a Wisdom saving throw. If the creature fails its saving throw, it is turned for 1 minute or until it takes any damage.
  1802|
  1803|A turned creature must spend its turns trying to move as far away from you as it can, and it can't willingly move to a space within 30 feet of you. It also can't take reactions. For its action, it can use only the Dash action or try to escape from an effect that prevents it from moving. If there's nowhere to move, the creature can use the Dodge action.
  1804|
  1805|### Ability Score Improvement
  1806|
  1807|When you reach 4th level, and again at 8th, 12th, 16th, and 19th level, you can increase one ability score of your choice by 2, or you can increase two ability scores of your choice by 1. As normal, you can't increase an ability score above 20 using this feature.
  1808|
  1809|### Destroy Undead
  1810|
  1811|Starting at 5th level, when an undead fails its saving throw against your Turn Undead feature, the creature is instantly destroyed if its challenge rating is at or below a certain threshold, as shown in the Destroy Undead table.
  1812|
  1813|
  1814|Destroy Undead
  1815|
  1816|
  1817|
  1818|
  1819|
  1820|
  1821|Cleric Level
  1822|Destroys Undead of CR…
  1823|
  1824|
  1825|
  1826|
  1827|5th
  1828|1/2 or lower
  1829|
  1830|
  1831|8th
  1832|1 or lower
  1833|
  1834|
  1835|11th
  1836|2 or lower
  1837|
  1838|
  1839|14th
  1840|3 or lower
  1841|
  1842|
  1843|17th
  1844|4 or lower
  1845|
  1846|
  1847|
  1848|
  1849|### Divine Intervention
  1850|
  1851|Beginning at 10th level, you can call on your deity to intervene on your behalf when your need is great.
  1852|
  1853|Imploring your deity's aid requires you to use your action. Describe the assistance you seek, and roll percentile dice. If you roll a number equal to or lower than your cleric level, your deity intervenes.
  1854|
  1855|The GM chooses the nature of the intervention; the effect of any cleric spell or cleric domain spell would be appropriate.
  1856|
  1857|If your deity intervenes, you can't use this feature again for 7 days. Otherwise, you can use it again after you finish a long rest.
  1858|
  1859|At 20th level, your call for intervention succeeds automatically, no roll required.
  1860|
  1861|## Domains {#section-domains}
  1862|
  1863|### Life Domain
  1864|
  1865|The Life domain focuses on the vibrant positive energy—one of the fundamental forces of the universe—that sustains all life. The gods of life (see "Pantheons") promote vitality and health through healing the sick and wounded, caring for those in need, and driving away the forces of death and undeath. Almost any non-evil deity can claim influence over this domain, particularly agricultural deities (such as Isis, The Daghdha, and Demeter), sun gods (such as Belenus, Frey, and Re-Horakhty), gods of healing or endurance (such as Apollo, Lugh, and Diancecht), and gods of home and community (such as Hestia, Hathor, and Frigga).
  1866|
  1867|
  1868|Life Domain Spells
  1869|
  1870|
  1871|
  1872|
  1873|
  1874|
  1875|Cleric Level
  1876|Spells
  1877|
  1878|
  1879|
  1880|
  1881|1st
  1882|bless, cure wounds
  1883|
  1884|
  1885|3rd
  1886|lesser restoration, spiritual weapon
  1887|
  1888|
  1889|5th
  1890|beacon of hope, revivify
  1891|
  1892|
  1893|7th
  1894|death ward, guardian of faith
  1895|
  1896|
  1897|9th
  1898|mass cure wounds, raise dead
  1899|
  1900|
  1901|
  1902|
  1903|#### Bonus Proficiency
  1904|
  1905|When you choose this domain at 1st level, you gain proficiency with heavy armor.
  1906|
  1907|#### Disciple of Life
  1908|
  1909|Also starting at 1st level, your healing spells are more effective. Whenever you use a spell of 1st level or higher to restore hit points to a creature, the creature regains additional hit points equal to 2 + the spell's level.
  1910|
  1911|#### Channel Divinity: Preserve Life
  1912|
  1913|Starting at 2nd level, you can use your Channel Divinity to heal the badly injured.
  1914|
  1915|As an action, you present your holy symbol and evoke healing energy that can restore a number of hit points equal to five times your cleric level.
  1916|
  1917|Choose any creatures within 30 feet of you, and divide those hit points among them. This feature can restore a creature to no more than half of its hit point maximum. You can't use this feature on an undead or a construct.
  1918|
  1919|#### Blessed Healer
  1920|
  1921|Beginning at 6th level, the healing spells you cast on others heal you as well. When you cast a spell of 1st level or higher that restores hit points to a creature other than you, you regain hit points equal to 2 + the spell's level.
  1922|
  1923|#### Divine Strike
  1924|
  1925|At 8th level, you gain the ability to infuse your weapon strikes with divine energy. Once on each of your turns when you hit a creature with a weapon attack, you can cause the attack to deal an extra 1d8 radiant damage to the target. When you reach 14th level, the extra damage increases to 2d8.
  1926|
  1927|#### Supreme Healing
  1928|
  1929|Starting at 17th level, when you would normally roll one or more dice to restore hit points with a spell, you instead use the highest number possible for each die. For example, instead of restoring 2d6 hit points to a creature, you restore 12.
  1930|

  2064|4
  2065|
  2066|
  2067|20th
  2068|+6
  2069|Archdruid
  2070|4
  2071|
  2072|
  2073|
  2074|
  2075|As a druid, you gain the following class features.
  2076|
  2077|#### Hit Points
  2078|
  2079|Hit Dice: 1d8 per druid level
  2080|
  2081|Hit Points at 1st Level: 8 + your Constitution modifier
  2082|
  2083|Hit Points at Higher Levels: 1d8 (or 5) + your Constitution modifier per druid level after 1st
  2084|
  2085|#### Proficiencies
  2086|
  2087|Armor: Light armor, medium armor, shields (druids will not wear armor or use shields made of metal)
  2088|
  2089|Weapons: Clubs, daggers, darts, javelins, maces, quarterstaffs, scimitars, sickles, slings, spears
  2090|
  2091|Tools: Herbalism kit
  2092|
  2093|Saving Throws: Intelligence, Wisdom
  2094|
  2095|Skills: Choose two from Arcana, Animal Handling, Insight, Medicine, Nature, Perception, Religion, and Survival
  2096|
  2097|#### Equipment
  2098|
  2099|You start with the following equipment, in addition to the equipment granted by your background:
  2100|
  2101|-   (a) a wooden shield or (b) any simple weapon
  2102|-   (a) a scimitar or (b) any simple melee weapon
  2103|-   Leather armor, an explorer's pack, and a druidic focus
  2104|
  2105|### Druidic
  2106|
  2107|You know Druidic, the secret language of druids. You can speak the language and use it to leave hidden messages. You and others who know this language automatically spot such a message. Others spot the message's presence with a successful DC 15 Wisdom (Perception) check but can't decipher it without magic.
  2108|
  2109|### Spellcasting
  2110|
  2111|Drawing on the divine essence of nature itself, you can cast spells to shape that essence to your will.
  2112|
  2113|#### Cantrips
  2114|
  2115|At 1st level, you know two cantrips of your choice from the druid spell list. You learn additional druid cantrips of your choice at higher levels, as shown in the Cantrips Known column of the Druid table.
  2116|
  2117|#### Preparing and Casting Spells
  2118|
  2119|
  2120|Druid Spell Slots per Level
  2121|
  2122|
  2123|
  2124|
  2125|
  2126|
  2127|
  2128|
  2129|
  2130|
  2131|
  2132|
  2133|

  2375|1
  2376|1
  2377|1
  2378|
  2379|
  2380|20th
  2381|4
  2382|3
  2383|3
  2384|3
  2385|3
  2386|2
  2387|2
  2388|1
  2389|1
  2390|
  2391|
  2392|
  2393|
  2394|The Druid Spell Slots per Level table shows how many spell slots you have to cast your spells of 1st level and higher. To cast one of these druid spells, you must expend a slot of the spell's level or higher. You regain all expended spell slots when you finish a long rest.
  2395|
  2396|You prepare the list of druid spells that are available for you to cast, choosing from the druid spell list. When you do so, choose a number of druid spells equal to your Wisdom modifier + your druid level (minimum of one spell). The spells must be of a level for which you have spell slots.
  2397|
  2398|For example, if you are a 3rd-level druid, you have four 1st-level and two 2nd-level spell slots. With a Wisdom of 16, your list of prepared spells can include six spells of 1st or 2nd level, in any combination. If you prepare the 1st-level spell cure wounds, you can cast it using a 1st-level or 2nd-level slot. Casting the spell doesn't remove it from your list of prepared spells.
  2399|
  2400|You can also change your list of prepared spells when you finish a long rest. Preparing a new list of druid spells requires time spent in prayer and meditation: at least 1 minute per spell level for each spell on your list.
  2401|
  2402|#### Spellcasting Ability
  2403|
  2404|Wisdom is your spellcasting ability for your druid spells, since your magic draws upon your devotion and attunement to nature. You use your Wisdom whenever a spell refers to your spellcasting ability. In addition, you use your Wisdom modifier when setting the saving throw DC for a druid spell you cast and when making an attack roll with one.
  2405|
  2406|
  2407|
  2408|
  2409|
  2410|
  2411|
  2412|Spell save DC = 8 + your proficiency bonus + your Wisdom modifier
  2413|
  2414|
  2415|Spell attack modifier = your proficiency bonus + your Wisdom modifier
  2416|
  2417|
  2418|
  2419|
  2420|#### Ritual Casting
  2421|
  2422|You can cast a druid spell as a ritual if that spell has the ritual tag and you have the spell prepared.
  2423|
  2424|#### Spellcasting Focus
  2425|
  2426|You can use a druidic focus (see "Equipment") as a spellcasting focus for your druid spells.
  2427|
  2428|### Wild Shape
  2429|
  2430|Starting at 2nd level, you can use your action to magically assume the shape of a beast that you have seen before. You can use this feature twice. You regain expended uses when you finish a short or long rest.
  2431|
  2432|Your druid level determines the beasts you can transform into, as shown in the Beast Shapes table. At 2nd level, for example, you can transform into any beast that has a challenge rating of 1/4 or lower that doesn't have a flying or swimming speed.
  2433|
  2434|
  2435|Beast Shapes
  2436|
  2437|
  2438|
  2439|
  2440|
  2441|
  2442|
  2443|
  2444|Level
  2445|Max. CR
  2446|Limitations
  2447|Example
  2448|
  2449|
  2450|
  2451|
  2452|2nd
  2453|1/4
  2454|No flying or swimming speed
  2455|Wolf
  2456|
  2457|
  2458|4th
  2459|1/2
  2460|No flying speed
  2461|Crocodile
  2462|
  2463|
  2464|8th
  2465|1
  2466|—
  2467|Giant eagle
  2468|
  2469|
  2470|
  2471|
  2472|You can stay in a beast shape for a number of hours equal to half your druid level (rounded down). You then revert to your normal form unless you expend another use of this feature. You can revert to your normal form earlier by using a bonus action on your turn. You automatically revert if you fall unconscious, drop to 0 hit points, or die.
  2473|
  2474|While you are transformed, the following rules apply:
  2475|
  2476|-   Your game statistics are replaced by the statistics of the beast, but you retain your alignment, personality, and Intelligence, Wisdom, and Charisma scores. You also retain all of your skill and saving throw proficiencies, in addition to gaining those of the creature. If the creature has the same proficiency as you and the bonus in its stat block is higher than yours, use the creature's bonus instead of yours. If the creature has any legendary or lair actions, you can't use them.
  2477|-   When you transform, you assume the beast's hit points and Hit Dice. When you revert to your normal form, you return to the number of hit points you had before you transformed. However, if you revert as a result of dropping to 0 hit points, any excess damage carries over to your normal form. For example, if you take 10 damage in animal form and have only 1 hit point left, you revert and take 9 damage. As long as the excess damage doesn't reduce your normal form to 0 hit points, you aren't knocked unconscious.
  2478|-   You can't cast spells, and your ability to speak or take any action that requires hands is limited to the capabilities of your beast form. Transforming doesn't break your concentration on a spell you've already cast, however, or prevent you from taking actions that are part of a spell, such as call lightning, that you've already cast.
  2479|-   You retain the benefit of any features from your class, race, or other source and can use them if the new form is physically capable of doing so. However, you can't use any of your special senses, such as darkvision, unless your new form also has that sense.
  2480|-   You choose whether your equipment falls to the ground in your space, merges into your new form, or is worn by it. Worn equipment functions as normal, but the GM decides whether it is practical for the new form to wear a piece of equipment, based on the creature's shape and size. Your equipment doesn't change size or shape to match the new form, and any equipment that the new form can't wear must either fall to the ground or merge with it. Equipment that merges with the form has no effect until you leave the form.
  2481|
  2482|### Druid Circle
  2483|
  2484|At 2nd level, you choose to identify with a circle of druids (see "Druid Circles"). Your choice grants you features at 2nd level and again at 6th, 10th, and 14th level.
  2485|
  2486|### Timeless Body
  2487|
  2488|Starting at 18th level, the primal magic that you wield causes you to age more slowly. For every 10 years that pass, your body ages only 1 year.
  2489|
  2490|### Ability Score Improvement
  2491|
  2492|When you reach 4th level, and again at 8th, 12th, 16th, and 19th level, you can increase one ability score of your choice by 2, or you can increase two ability scores of your choice by 1. As normal, you can't increase an ability score above 20 using this feature.
  2493|
  2494|### Beast Spells
  2495|
  2496|Beginning at 18th level, you can cast many of your druid spells in any shape you assume using Wild Shape. You can perform the somatic and verbal components of a druid spell while in a beast shape, but you aren't able to provide material components.
  2497|
  2498|### Archdruid
  2499|
  2500|At 20th level, you can use your Wild Shape an unlimited number of times.
  2501|
  2502|Additionally, you can ignore the verbal and somatic components of your druid spells, as well as any material components that lack a cost and aren't consumed by a spell. You gain this benefit in both your normal shape and your beast shape from Wild Shape.
  2503|
  2504|> #### Sacred Plants and Wood
  2505|>
  2506|> A druid holds certain plants to be sacred, particularly alder, ash, birch, elder, hazel, holly, juniper, mistletoe, oak, rowan, willow, and yew. Druids often use such plants as part of a spellcasting focus, incorporating lengths of oak or yew or sprigs of mistletoe.
  2507|>
  2508|> Similarly, a druid uses such woods to make other objects, such as weapons and shields. Yew is associated with death and rebirth, so weapon handles for scimitars or sickles might be fashioned from it. Ash is associated with life and oak with strength. These woods make excellent hafts or whole weapons, such as clubs or quarterstaffs, as well as shields. Alder is associated with air, and it might be used for thrown weapons, such as darts or javelins.
  2509|>
  2510|> Druids from regions that lack the plants described here have chosen other plants to take on similar uses. For instance, a druid of a desert region might value the yucca tree and cactus plants.
  2511|
  2512|> #### Druids and the Gods
  2513|>
  2514|> Some druids venerate the forces of nature themselves, but most druids are devoted to one of the many nature deities worshiped in the multiverse (the lists of gods in the "Pantheons" section include many such deities). The worship of these deities is often considered a more ancient tradition than the faiths of clerics and urbanized peoples.
  2515|
  2516|## Druid Circles {#section-druid-circles}
  2517|
  2518|### Circle of the Land
  2519|
  2520|The Circle of the Land is made up of mystics and sages who safeguard ancient knowledge and rites through a vast oral tradition. These druids meet within sacred circles of trees or standing stones to whisper primal secrets in Druidic. The circle's wisest members preside as the chief priests of communities that hold to the Old Faith and serve as advisors to the rulers of those folk. As a member of this circle, your magic is influenced by the land where you were initiated into the circle's mysterious rites.
  2521|
  2522|#### Bonus Cantrip
  2523|
  2524|When you choose this circle at 2nd level, you learn one additional druid cantrip of your choice.
  2525|
  2526|#### Natural Recovery
  2527|
  2528|Starting at 2nd level, you can regain some of your magical energy by sitting in meditation and communing with nature. During a short rest, you choose expended spell slots to recover. The spell slots can have a combined level that is equal to or less than half your druid level (rounded up), and none of the slots can be 6th level or higher. You can't use this feature again until you finish a long rest.
  2529|
  2530|For example, when you are a 4th-level druid, you can recover up to two levels worth of spell slots. You can recover either a 2nd-level slot or two 1st-level slots.
  2531|
  2532|#### Circle Spells
  2533|
  2534|Your mystical connection to the land infuses you with the ability to cast certain spells. At 3rd, 5th, 7th, and 9th level you gain access to circle spells connected to the land where you became a druid. Choose that land—arctic, coast, desert, forest, grassland, mountain, or swamp—and consult the associated list of spells.
  2535|
  2536|Once you gain access to a circle spell, you always have it prepared, and it doesn't count against the number of spells you can prepare each day. If you gain access to a spell that doesn't appear on the druid spell list, the spell is nonetheless a druid spell for you.

  2759|
  2760|
  2761|
  2762|#### Land's Stride
  2763|
  2764|Starting at 6th level, moving through nonmagical difficult terrain costs you no extra movement. You can also pass through nonmagical plants without being slowed by them and without taking damage from them if they have thorns, spines, or a similar hazard.
  2765|
  2766|In addition, you have advantage on saving throws against plants that are magically created or manipulated to impede movement, such those created by the entangle spell.
  2767|
  2768|#### Nature's Ward
  2769|
  2770|When you reach 10th level, you can't be charmed or frightened by elementals or fey, and you are immune to poison and disease.
  2771|
  2772|#### Nature's Sanctuary
  2773|
  2774|When you reach 14th level, creatures of the natural world sense your connection to nature and become hesitant to attack you. When a beast or plant creature attacks you, that creature must make a Wisdom saving throw against your druid spell save DC. On a failed save, the creature must choose a different target, or the attack automatically misses. On a successful save, the creature is immune to this effect for 24 hours.
  2775|
  2776|The creature is aware of this effect before it makes its attack against you.
  2777|
  2778|## Fighter {#section-fighter}
  2779|
  2780|### Class Features
  2781|
  2782|
  2783|The Fighter

  2895|Extra Attack (3)
  2896|
  2897|
  2898|
  2899|
  2900|As a fighter, you gain the following class features.
  2901|
  2902|#### Hit Points
  2903|
  2904|Hit Dice: 1d10 per fighter level
  2905|
  2906|Hit Points at 1st Level: 10 + your Constitution modifier
  2907|
  2908|Hit Points at Higher Levels: 1d10 (or 6) + your Constitution modifier per fighter level after 1st
  2909|
  2910|#### Proficiencies
  2911|
  2912|Armor: All armor, shields
  2913|
  2914|Weapons: Simple weapons, martial weapons
  2915|
  2916|Tools: None
  2917|
  2918|Saving Throws: Strength, Constitution
  2919|
  2920|Skills: Choose two skills from Acrobatics, Animal Handling, Athletics, History, Insight, Intimidation, Perception, and Survival
  2921|
  2922|#### Equipment
  2923|
  2924|You start with the following equipment, in addition to the equipment granted by your background:
  2925|
  2926|-   (a) chain mail or (b) leather armor, longbow, and 20 arrows
  2927|-   (a) a martial weapon and a shield or (b) two martial weapons
  2928|-   (a) a light crossbow and 20 bolts or (b) two handaxes
  2929|-   (a) a dungeoneer's pack or (b) an explorer's pack
  2930|
  2931|### Fighting Style
  2932|
  2933|You adopt a particular style of fighting as your specialty. Choose one of the following options. You can't take a Fighting Style option more than once, even if you later get to choose again.
  2934|
  2935|#### Archery
  2936|
  2937|You gain a +2 bonus to attack rolls you make with ranged weapons.
  2938|
  2939|#### Defense
  2940|
  2941|While you are wearing armor, you gain a +1 bonus to AC.
  2942|
  2943|#### Dueling
  2944|
  2945|When you are wielding a melee weapon in one hand and no other weapons, you gain a +2 bonus to damage rolls with that weapon.
  2946|
  2947|#### Great Weapon Fighting
  2948|
  2949|When you roll a 1 or 2 on a damage die for an attack you make with a melee weapon that you are wielding with two hands, you can reroll the die and must use the new roll, even if the new roll is a 1 or a 2. The weapon must have the two-handed or versatile property for you to gain this benefit.
  2950|
  2951|#### Protection
  2952|
  2953|When a creature you can see attacks a target other than you that is within 5 feet of you, you can use your reaction to impose disadvantage on the attack roll. You must be wielding a shield.
  2954|
  2955|#### Two-Weapon Fighting
  2956|
  2957|When you engage in two-weapon fighting, you can add your ability modifier to the damage of the second attack.
  2958|
  2959|### Second Wind
  2960|
  2961|You have a limited well of stamina that you can draw on to protect yourself from harm. On your turn, you can use a bonus action to regain hit points equal to 1d10 + your fighter level. Once you use this feature, you must finish a short or long rest before you can use it again.
  2962|
  2963|### Action Surge
  2964|
  2965|Starting at 2nd level, you can push yourself beyond your normal limits for a moment. On your turn, you can take one additional action on top of your regular action and a possible bonus action. Once you use this feature, you must finish a short or long rest before you can use it again. Starting at 17th level, you can use it twice before a rest, but only once on the same turn.
  2966|
  2967|### Martial Archetype
  2968|
  2969|At 3rd level, you choose an archetype (see "Martial Archetypes") that you strive to emulate in your combat styles and techniques. The archetype you choose grants you features at 3rd level and again at 7th, 10th, 15th, and 18th level.
  2970|
  2971|### Ability Score Improvement
  2972|
  2973|When you reach 4th level, and again at 6th, 8th, 12th, 14th, 16th, and 19th level, you can increase one ability score of your choice by 2, or you can increase two ability scores of your choice by 1. As normal, you can't increase an ability score above 20 using this feature.
  2974|
  2975|### Extra Attack
  2976|
  2977|Beginning at 5th level, you can attack twice, instead of once, whenever you take the Attack action on your turn.
  2978|
  2979|The number of attacks increases to three when you reach 11th level in this class and to four when you reach 20th level in this class.
  2980|
  2981|### Indomitable
  2982|
  2983|Beginning at 9th level, you can reroll a saving throw that you fail. If you do so, you must use the new roll, and you can't use this feature again until you finish a long rest.
  2984|
  2985|You can use this feature twice between long rests starting at 13th level and three times between long rests starting at 17th level.
  2986|
  2987|## Martial Archetypes {#section-martial-archetypes}
  2988|
  2989|Different fighters choose different approaches to perfecting their fighting prowess. The martial archetype you choose to emulate reflects your approach.
  2990|
  2991|### Champion
  2992|
  2993|The archetypal Champion focuses on the development of raw physical power honed to deadly perfection. Those who model themselves on this archetype combine rigorous training with physical excellence to deal devastating blows.
  2994|
  2995|#### Improved Critical
  2996|
  2997|Beginning when you choose this archetype at 3rd level, your weapon attacks score a critical hit on a roll of 19 or 20.
  2998|
  2999|#### Remarkable Athlete
  3000|
  3001|Starting at 7th level, you can add half your proficiency bonus (round up) to any Strength, Dexterity, or Constitution check you make that doesn't already use your proficiency bonus.
  3002|
  3003|In addition, when you make a running long jump, the distance you can cover increases by a number of feet equal to your Strength modifier.
  3004|
  3005|#### Additional Fighting Style
  3006|
  3007|At 10th level, you can choose a second option from the Fighting Style class feature.
  3008|
  3009|#### Superior Critical
  3010|
  3011|Starting at 15th level, your weapon attacks score a critical hit on a roll of 18–20.
  3012|
  3013|#### Survivor
  3014|
  3015|At 18th level, you attain the pinnacle of resilience in battle. At the start of each of your turns, you regain hit points equal to 5 + your Constitution modifier if you have no more than half of your hit points left. You don't gain this benefit if you have 0 hit points.
  3016|
  3017|## Monk {#section-monk}
  3018|
  3019|### Class Features
  3020|
  3021|
  3022|The Monk
  3023|
  3024|
  3025|
  3026|
  3027|
  3028|
  3029|
  3030|
  3031|
  3032|

  3035|Martial Arts
  3036|Ki Points
  3037|Unarmored Movement
  3038|Features
  3039|
  3040|
  3041|
  3042|
  3043|1st
  3044|+2
  3045|1d4
  3046|—
  3047|—
  3048|Unarmored Defense, Martial Arts
  3049|
  3050|

  3053|1d4
  3054|2
  3055|+10 ft.
  3056|Ki, Unarmored Movement
  3057|
  3058|
  3059|3rd
