  3111|+15 ft.
  3112|Unarmored Movement improvement
  3113|
  3114|
  3115|10th
  3116|+4
  3117|1d6
  3118|10
  3119|+20 ft.
  3120|Purity of Body
  3121|
  3122|
  3123|11th
  3124|+4
  3125|1d8
  3126|11
  3127|+20 ft.
  3128|Monastic Tradition feature
  3129|
  3130|
  3131|12th
  3132|+4
  3133|1d8
  3134|12
  3135|+20 ft.
  3136|Ability Score Improvement
  3137|
  3138|
  3139|13th
  3140|+5
  3141|1d8
  3142|13
  3143|+20 ft.
  3144|Tongue of the Sun and Moon
  3145|
  3146|
  3147|14th
  3148|+5
  3149|1d8
  3150|14
  3151|+25 ft.
  3152|Diamond Soul
  3153|
  3154|
  3155|15th
  3156|+5
  3157|1d8
  3158|15
  3159|+25 ft.
  3160|Timeless Body
  3161|
  3162|
  3163|16th
  3164|+5
  3165|1d8
  3166|16
  3167|+25 ft.
  3168|Ability Score Improvement
  3169|
  3170|
  3171|17th
  3172|+6
  3173|1d10
  3174|17
  3175|+25 ft.
  3176|Monastic Tradition feature
  3177|
  3178|
  3179|18th
  3180|+6
  3181|1d10
  3182|18
  3183|+30 ft.
  3184|Empty Body
  3185|
  3186|
  3187|19th
  3188|+6
  3189|1d10
  3190|19
  3191|+30 ft.
  3192|Ability Score Improvement
  3193|
  3194|
  3195|20th
  3196|+6
  3197|1d10
  3198|20
  3199|+30 ft.
  3200|Perfect Self
  3201|
  3202|
  3203|
  3204|
  3205|As a monk, you gain the following class features.
  3206|
  3207|#### Hit Points
  3208|
  3209|Hit Dice: 1d8 per monk level
  3210|
  3211|Hit Points at 1st Level: 8 + your Constitution modifier
  3212|
  3213|Hit Points at Higher Levels: 1d8 (or 5) + your Constitution modifier per monk level after 1st
  3214|
  3215|#### Proficiencies
  3216|
  3217|Armor: None
  3218|
  3219|Weapons: Simple weapons, shortswords
  3220|
  3221|Tools: Choose one type of artisan's tools or one musical instrument
  3222|
  3223|Saving Throws: Strength, Dexterity
  3224|
  3225|Skills: Choose two from Acrobatics, Athletics, History, Insight, Religion, and Stealth
  3226|
  3227|#### Equipment
  3228|
  3229|You start with the following equipment, in addition to the equipment granted by your background:
  3230|
  3231|-   (a) a shortsword or (b) any simple weapon
  3232|-   (a) a dungeoneer's pack or (b) an explorer's pack
  3233|-   10 darts
  3234|
  3235|### Unarmored Defense
  3236|
  3237|Beginning at 1st level, while you are wearing no armor and not wielding a shield, your AC equals 10 + your Dexterity modifier + your Wisdom modifier.
  3238|
  3239|### Martial Arts
  3240|
  3241|At 1st level, your practice of martial arts gives you mastery of combat styles that use unarmed strikes and monk weapons, which are shortswords and any simple melee weapons that don't have the two-handed or heavy property.
  3242|
  3243|You gain the following benefits while you are unarmed or wielding only monk weapons and you aren't wearing armor or wielding a shield:
  3244|
  3245|-   You can use Dexterity instead of Strength for the attack and damage rolls of your unarmed strikes and monk weapons.
  3246|-   You can roll a d4 in place of the normal damage of your unarmed strike or monk weapon. This die changes as you gain monk levels, as shown in the Martial Arts column of the Monk table.
  3247|-   When you use the Attack action with an unarmed strike or a monk weapon on your turn, you can make one unarmed strike as a bonus action. For example, if you take the Attack action and attack with a quarterstaff, you can also make an unarmed strike as a bonus action, assuming you haven't already taken a bonus action this turn.
  3248|
  3249|Certain monasteries use specialized forms of the monk weapons. For example, you might use a club that is two lengths of wood connected by a short chain (called a nunchaku) or a sickle with a shorter, straighter blade (called a kama). Whatever name you use for a monk weapon, you can use the game statistics provided for the weapon.
  3250|
  3251|### Ki
  3252|
  3253|Starting at 2nd level, your training allows you to harness the mystic energy of ki. Your access to this energy is represented by a number of ki points. Your monk level determines the number of points you have, as shown in the Ki Points column of the Monk table.
  3254|
  3255|You can spend these points to fuel various ki features. You start knowing three such features: Flurry of Blows, Patient Defense, and Step of the Wind. You learn more ki features as you gain levels in this class.
  3256|
  3257|When you spend a ki point, it is unavailable until you finish a short or long rest, at the end of which you draw all of your expended ki back into yourself. You must spend at least 30 minutes of the rest meditating to regain your ki points.
  3258|
  3259|Some of your ki features require your target to make a saving throw to resist the feature's effects. The saving throw DC is calculated as follows:
  3260|
  3261||                                                                    |
  3262||:------------------------------------------------------------------:|
  3263|| Ki save DC = 8 + your proficiency bonus + your Wisdom modifier |
  3264|
  3265|#### Flurry of Blows
  3266|
  3267|Immediately after you take the Attack action on your turn, you can spend 1 ki point to make two unarmed strikes as a bonus action.
  3268|
  3269|#### Patient Defense
  3270|
  3271|You can spend 1 ki point to take the Dodge action as a bonus action on your turn.
  3272|
  3273|#### Step of the Wind
  3274|
  3275|You can spend 1 ki point to take the Disengage or Dash action as a bonus action on your turn, and your jump distance is doubled for the turn.
  3276|
  3277|### Unarmored Movement
  3278|
  3279|Starting at 2nd level, your speed increases by 10 feet while you are not wearing armor or wielding a shield. This bonus increases when you reach certain monk levels, as shown in the Monk table.
  3280|
  3281|At 9th level, you gain the ability to move along vertical surfaces and across liquids on your turn without falling during the move.
  3282|
  3283|### Monastic Tradition
  3284|
  3285|When you reach 3rd level, you commit yourself to a monastic tradition (see "Monastic Traditions"). Your tradition grants you features at 3rd level and again at 6th, 11th, and 17th level.
  3286|
  3287|### Deflect Missiles
  3288|
  3289|Starting at 3rd level, you can use your reaction to deflect or catch the missile when you are hit by a ranged weapon attack. When you do so, the damage you take from the attack is reduced by 1d10 + your Dexterity modifier + your monk level.
  3290|
  3291|If you reduce the damage to 0, you can catch the missile if it is small enough for you to hold in one hand and you have at least one hand free. If you catch a missile in this way, you can spend 1 ki point to make a ranged attack with the weapon or piece of ammunition you just caught, as part of the same reaction. You make this attack with proficiency, regardless of your weapon proficiencies, and the missile counts as a monk weapon for the attack, which has a normal range of 20 feet and a long range of 60 feet.
  3292|
  3293|### Ability Score Improvement
  3294|
  3295|When you reach 4th level, and again at 8th, 12th, 16th, and 19th level, you can increase one ability score of your choice by 2, or you can increase two ability scores of your choice by 1. As normal, you can't increase an ability score above 20 using this feature.
  3296|
  3297|### Slow Fall
  3298|
  3299|Beginning at 4th level, you can use your reaction when you fall to reduce any falling damage you take by an amount equal to five times your monk level.
  3300|
  3301|### Extra Attack
  3302|
  3303|Beginning at 5th level, you can attack twice, instead of once, whenever you take the Attack action on your turn.
  3304|
  3305|### Timeless Body
  3306|
  3307|At 15th level, your ki sustains you so that you suffer none of the frailty of old age, and you can't be aged magically. You can still die of old age, however. In addition, you no longer need food or water.
  3308|
  3309|### Stunning Strike
  3310|
  3311|Starting at 5th level, you can interfere with the flow of ki in an opponent's body. When you hit another creature with a melee weapon attack, you can spend 1 ki point to attempt a stunning strike. The target must succeed on a Constitution saving throw or be stunned until the end of your next turn.
  3312|
  3313|### Ki-Empowered Strikes
  3314|
  3315|Starting at 6th level, your unarmed strikes count as magical for the purpose of overcoming resistance and immunity to nonmagical attacks and damage.
  3316|
  3317|### Evasion
  3318|
  3319|At 7th level, your instinctive agility lets you dodge out of the way of certain area effects, such as a blue dragon's lightning breath or a fireball spell. When you are subjected to an effect that allows you to make a Dexterity saving throw to take only half damage, you instead take no damage if you succeed on the saving throw, and only half damage if you fail.
  3320|
  3321|### Stillness of Mind
  3322|
  3323|Starting at 7th level, you can use your action to end one effect on yourself that is causing you to be charmed or frightened.
  3324|
  3325|### Purity of Body
  3326|
  3327|At 10th level, your mastery of the ki flowing through you makes you immune to disease and poison.
  3328|
  3329|### Tongue of the Sun and Moon
  3330|
  3331|Starting at 13th level, you learn to touch the ki of other minds so that you understand all spoken languages. Moreover, any creature that can understand a language can understand what you say.
  3332|
  3333|### Diamond Soul
  3334|
  3335|Beginning at 14th level, your mastery of ki grants you proficiency in all saving throws.
  3336|
  3337|Additionally, whenever you make a saving throw and fail, you can spend 1 ki point to reroll it and take the second result.
  3338|
  3339|### Empty Body
  3340|
  3341|Beginning at 18th level, you can use your action to spend 4 ki points to become invisible for 1 minute. During that time, you also have resistance to all damage but force damage.
  3342|
  3343|Additionally, you can spend 8 ki points to cast the astral projection spell, without needing material components. When you do so, you can't take any other creatures with you.
  3344|
  3345|### Perfect Self
  3346|
  3347|At 20th level, when you roll for initiative and have no ki points remaining, you regain 4 ki points.
  3348|
  3349|## Monastic Traditions {#section-monastic-traditions}
  3350|
  3351|Several traditions of monastic pursuit are common in the monasteries scattered across the multiverse.
  3352|
  3353|Most monasteries practice one tradition exclusively, but a few honor the many traditions and instruct each monk according to his or her aptitude and interest. All traditions rely on the same basic techniques, diverging as the student grows more adept. Thus, a monk need choose a tradition only upon reaching 3rd level.
  3354|
  3355|### Way of the Open Hand
  3356|
  3357|Monks of the Way of the Open Hand are the ultimate masters of martial arts combat, whether armed or unarmed. They learn techniques to push and trip their opponents, manipulate ki to heal damage to their bodies, and practice advanced meditation that can protect them from harm.
  3358|
  3359|#### Open Hand Technique
  3360|
  3361|Starting when you choose this tradition at 3rd level, you can manipulate your enemy's ki when you harness your own. Whenever you hit a creature with one of the attacks granted by your Flurry of Blows, you can impose one of the following effects on that target:
  3362|
  3363|-   It must succeed on a Dexterity saving throw or be knocked prone.
  3364|-   It must make a Strength saving throw. If it fails, you can push it up to 15 feet away from you.
  3365|-   It can't take reactions until the end of your next turn.
  3366|
  3367|#### Wholeness of Body
  3368|
  3369|At 6th level, you gain the ability to heal yourself. As an action, you can regain hit points equal to three times your monk level. You must finish a long rest before you can use this feature again.
  3370|
  3371|#### Tranquility
  3372|
  3373|Beginning at 11th level, you can enter a special meditation that surrounds you with an aura of peace. At the end of a long rest, you gain the effect of a sanctuary spell that lasts until the start of your next long rest (the spell can end early as normal). The saving throw DC for the spell equals 8 + your Wisdom modifier + your proficiency bonus.
  3374|
  3375|#### Quivering Palm
  3376|
  3377|At 17th level, you gain the ability to set up lethal vibrations in someone's body. When you hit a creature with an unarmed strike, you can spend 3 ki points to start these imperceptible vibrations, which last for a number of days equal to your monk level. The vibrations are harmless unless you use your action to end them. To do so, you and the target must be on the same plane of existence. When you use this action, the creature must make a Constitution saving throw. If it fails, it is reduced to 0 hit points. If it succeeds, it takes 10d10 necrotic damage.
  3378|
  3379|You can have only one creature under the effect of this feature at a time. You can choose to end the vibrations harmlessly without using an action.
  3380|
  3381|## Paladin {#section-paladin}
  3382|
  3383|### Class Features
  3384|
  3385|
  3386|The Paladin
  3387|
  3388|
  3389|
  3390|
  3391|
  3392|
  3393|

  3395|Proficiency Bonus
  3396|Features
  3397|

  3400|

  3402|+2
  3403|Divine Sense, Lay on Hands
  3404|
  3405|
  3406|2nd

  3408|Fighting Style, Spellcasting, Divine Spite
  3409|

  3434|

  3444|
  3445|
  3446|10th

  3448|Aura of Courage
  3449|
  3450|

  3453|Improved Divine Smite
  3454|

  3458|Ability Score Improvement

  3466|14th

  3468|Cleansing Touch
  3469|
  3470|
  3471|15th
  3472|+5
  3473|Sacred Oath feature
  3474|
  3475|
  3476|16th
  3477|+5
  3478|Ability Score Improvement
  3479|
  3480|
  3481|17th
  3482|+6
  3483|—
  3484|
  3485|
  3486|18th
  3487|+6
  3488|Aura improvements
  3489|
  3490|
  3491|19th
  3492|+6
  3493|Ability Score Improvement
  3494|
  3495|
  3496|20th
  3497|+6
  3498|Sacred Oath feature
  3499|
  3500|
  3501|
  3502|
  3503|As a paladin, you gain the following class features.
  3504|
  3505|#### Hit Points
  3506|
  3507|Hit Dice: 1d10 per paladin level
  3508|
  3509|Hit Points at 1st Level: 10 + your Constitution modifier
  3510|
  3511|Hit Points at Higher Levels: 1d10 (or 6) + your Constitution modifier per paladin level after 1st
  3512|
  3513|#### Proficiencies
  3514|
  3515|Armor: All armor, shields
  3516|
  3517|Weapons: Simple weapons, martial weapons
  3518|
  3519|Tools: None
  3520|
  3521|Saving Throws: Wisdom, Charisma
  3522|
  3523|Skills: Choose two from Athletics, Insight, Intimidation, Medicine, Persuasion, and Religion
  3524|
  3525|#### Equipment
  3526|
  3527|You start with the following equipment, in addition to the equipment granted by your background:
  3528|
  3529|-   (a) a martial weapon and a shield or (b) two martial weapons
  3530|-   (a) five javelins or (b) any simple melee weapon
  3531|-   (a) a priest's pack or (b) an explorer's pack
  3532|-   Chain mail and a holy symbol
  3533|
  3534|### Divine Sense
  3535|
  3536|The presence of strong evil registers on your senses like a noxious odor, and powerful good rings like heavenly music in your ears. As an action, you can open your awareness to detect such forces. Until the end of your next turn, you know the location of any celestial, fiend, or undead within 60 feet of you that is not behind total cover. You know the type (celestial, fiend, or undead) of any being whose presence you sense, but not its identity (the vampire Count Dracula, for instance). Within the same radius, you also detect the presence of any place or object that has been consecrated or desecrated, as with the hallow spell.
  3537|
  3538|You can use this feature a number of times equal to 1 + your Charisma modifier. When you finish a long rest, you regain all expended uses.
  3539|
  3540|### Lay on Hands
  3541|
  3542|Your blessed touch can heal wounds. You have a pool of healing power that replenishes when you take a long rest. With that pool, you can restore a total number of hit points equal to your paladin level × 5.
  3543|
  3544|As an action, you can touch a creature and draw power from the pool to restore a number of hit points to that creature, up to the maximum amount remaining in your pool.
  3545|
  3546|Alternatively, you can expend 5 hit points from your pool of healing to cure the target of one disease or neutralize one poison affecting it. You can cure multiple diseases and neutralize multiple poisons with a single use of Lay on Hands, expending hit points separately for each one.
  3547|
  3548|This feature has no effect on undead and constructs.
  3549|
  3550|### Fighting Style
  3551|
  3552|At 2nd level, you adopt a style of fighting as your specialty. Choose one of the following options. You can't take a Fighting Style option more than once, even if you later get to choose again.
  3553|
  3554|#### Defense
  3555|
  3556|While you are wearing armor, you gain a +1 bonus to AC.
  3557|
  3558|#### Dueling
  3559|
  3560|When you are wielding a melee weapon in one hand and no other weapons, you gain a +2 bonus to damage rolls with that weapon.
  3561|
  3562|#### Great Weapon Fighting
  3563|
  3564|When you roll a 1 or 2 on a damage die for an attack you make with a melee weapon that you are wielding with two hands, you can reroll the die and must use the new roll. The weapon must have the two-handed or versatile property for you to gain this benefit.
  3565|
  3566|#### Protection
  3567|
  3568|When a creature you can see attacks a target other than you that is within 5 feet of you, you can use your reaction to impose disadvantage on the attack roll. You must be wielding a shield.
  3569|
  3570|### Spellcasting
  3571|
  3572|By 2nd level, you have learned to draw on divine magic through meditation and prayer to cast spells as a cleric does.
  3573|
  3574|#### Preparing and Casting Spells
  3575|
  3576|
  3577|Paladin Spell Slots per Level
  3578|
  3579|
  3580|
  3581|
  3582|
  3583|
  3584|
  3585|
  3586|
  3587|
  3588|Paladin Level
  3589|Spell Level
  3590|
  3591|
  3592|1st
  3593|2nd
  3594|3rd
  3595|4th
  3596|5th
  3597|
  3598|
  3599|
  3600|
  3601|1st
  3602|—
  3603|—
  3604|—
  3605|—
  3606|—
  3607|
  3608|
  3609|2nd
  3610|2

  3737|18th

  3744|
  3745|19th
  3746|4
  3747|3
  3748|3
  3749|3
  3750|2
  3751|
  3752|
  3753|20th
  3754|4
  3755|3
  3756|3
  3757|3
  3758|2
  3759|
  3760|
  3761|
  3762|
  3763|The Paladin Spell Slots by Level table shows how many spell slots you have to cast your spells. To cast one of your paladin spells of 1st level or higher, you must expend a slot of the spell's level or higher. You regain all expended spell slots when you finish a long rest.
  3764|
  3765|You prepare the list of paladin spells that are available for you to cast, choosing from the paladin spell list. When you do so, choose a number of paladin spells equal to your Charisma modifier + half your paladin level, rounded down (minimum of one spell). The spells must be of a level for which you have spell slots.
  3766|
  3767|For example, if you are a 5th-level paladin, you have four 1st-level and two 2nd-level spell slots.
  3768|
  3769|With a Charisma of 14, your list of prepared spells can include four spells of 1st or 2nd level, in any combination. If you prepare the 1st-level spell cure wounds, you can cast it using a 1st-level or a 2nd-level slot. Casting the spell doesn't remove it from your list of prepared spells.
  3770|
  3771|You can change your list of prepared spells when you finish a long rest. Preparing a new list of paladin spells requires time spent in prayer and meditation: at least 1 minute per spell level for each spell on your list.
  3772|
  3773|#### Spellcasting Ability
  3774|
  3775|Charisma is your spellcasting ability for your paladin spells, since their power derives from the strength of your convictions. You use your Charisma whenever a spell refers to your spellcasting ability. In addition, you use your Charisma modifier when setting the saving throw DC for a paladin spell you cast and when making an attack roll with one.
  3776|
  3777|
  3778|
  3779|
  3780|
  3781|
  3782|
  3783|Spell save DC = 8 + your proficiency bonus + your Charisma modifier
  3784|
  3785|
  3786|Spell attack modifier = your proficiency bonus + your Charisma modifier
  3787|
  3788|
  3789|
  3790|
  3791|### Spellcasting Focus
  3792|
  3793|You can use a holy symbol as a spellcasting focus for your paladin spells.
  3794|
  3795|### Divine Smite
  3796|
  3797|Starting at 2nd level, when you hit a creature with a melee weapon attack, you can expend one spell slot to deal radiant damage to the target, in addition to the weapon's damage. The extra damage is 2d8 for a 1st-level spell slot, plus 1d8 for each spell level higher than 1st, to a maximum of 5d8. The damage increases by 1d8 if the target is an undead or a fiend.
  3798|
  3799|### Divine Health
  3800|
  3801|By 3rd level, the divine magic flowing through you makes you immune to disease.
  3802|
  3803|### Sacred Oath
  3804|
  3805|When you reach 3rd level, you swear the oath (see "Sacred Oaths") that binds you as a paladin forever. Up to this time you have been in a preparatory stage, committed to the path but not yet sworn to it. Now you choose a sacred oath.
  3806|
  3807|Your choice grants you features at 3rd level and again at 7th, 15th, and 20th level. Those features include oath spells and the Channel Divinity feature.
  3808|
  3809|#### Oath Spells
  3810|
  3811|Each oath has a list of associated spells. You gain access to these spells at the levels specified in the oath description. Once you gain access to an oath spell, you always have it prepared. Oath spells don't count against the number of spells you can prepare each day.
  3812|
  3813|If you gain an oath spell that doesn't appear on the paladin spell list, the spell is nonetheless a paladin spell for you.
  3814|
  3815|#### Channel Divinity
  3816|
  3817|Your oath allows you to channel divine energy to fuel magical effects. Each Channel Divinity option provided by your oath explains how to use it.
  3818|
  3819|When you use your Channel Divinity, you choose which option to use. You must then finish a short or long rest to use your Channel Divinity again.
  3820|
  3821|Some Channel Divinity effects require saving throws. When you use such an effect from this class, the DC equals your paladin spell save DC.
  3822|
  3823|### Ability Score Improvement
  3824|
  3825|When you reach 4th level, and again at 8th, 12th, 16th, and 19th level, you can increase one ability score of your choice by 2, or you can increase two ability scores of your choice by 1. As normal, you can't increase an ability score above 20 using this feature.
  3826|
  3827|### Extra Attack
  3828|
  3829|Beginning at 5th level, you can attack twice, instead of once, whenever you take the Attack action on your turn.
  3830|
  3831|### Aura of Protection
  3832|
  3833|Starting at 6th level, whenever you or a friendly creature within 10 feet of you must make a saving throw, the creature gains a bonus to the saving throw equal to your Charisma modifier (with a minimum bonus of +1). You must be conscious to grant this bonus.
  3834|
  3835|At 18th level, the range of this aura increases to 30 feet.
  3836|
  3837|### Aura of Courage
  3838|
  3839|Starting at 10th level, you and friendly creatures within 10 feet of you can't be frightened while you are conscious.
  3840|
  3841|At 18th level, the range of this aura increases to 30 feet.
  3842|
  3843|### Improved Divine Smite
  3844|
  3845|By 11th level, you are so suffused with righteous might that all your melee weapon strikes carry divine power with them. Whenever you hit a creature with a melee weapon, the creature takes an extra 1d8 radiant damage. If you also use your Divine Smite with an attack, you add this damage to the extra damage of your Divine Smite.
  3846|
  3847|### Cleansing Touch
  3848|
  3849|Beginning at 14th level, you can use your action to end one spell on yourself or on one willing creature that you touch.
  3850|
  3851|You can use this feature a number of times equal to your Charisma modifier (a minimum of once). You regain expended uses when you finish a long rest.
  3852|
  3853|## Sacred Oaths {#section-sacred-oaths}
  3854|
  3855|Becoming a paladin involves taking vows that commit the paladin to the cause of righteousness, an active path of fighting wickedness. The final oath, taken when he or she reaches 3rd level, is the culmination of all the paladin's training. Some characters with this class don't consider themselves true paladins until they have reached 3rd level and made this oath. For others, the actual swearing of the oath is a formality, an official stamp on what has always been true in the paladin's heart.
  3856|
  3857|> #### Breaking Your Oath
  3858|>
  3859|> A paladin tries to hold to the highest standards of conduct, but even the most virtuous paladin is fallible. Sometimes the right path proves too demanding, sometimes a situation calls for the lesser of two evils, and sometimes the heat of emotion causes a paladin to transgress his or her oath.
  3860|>
  3861|> A paladin who has broken a vow typically seeks absolution from a cleric who shares his or her faith or from another paladin of the same order. The paladin might spend an all- night vigil in prayer as a sign of penitence, or undertake a fast or similar act of self-denial. After a rite of confession and forgiveness, the paladin starts fresh.
  3862|>
  3863|> If a paladin willfully violates his or her oath and shows no sign of repentance, the consequences can be more serious. At the GM's discretion, an impenitent paladin might be forced to abandon this class and adopt another.
  3864|
  3865|### Oath of Devotion
  3866|
  3867|The Oath of Devotion binds a paladin to the loftiest ideals of justice, virtue, and order. Sometimes called cavaliers, white knights, or holy warriors, these paladins meet the ideal of the knight in shining armor, acting with honor in pursuit of justice and the greater good. They hold themselves to the highest standards of conduct, and some, for better or worse, hold the rest of the world to the same standards. Many who swear this oath are devoted to gods of law and good and use their gods' tenets as the measure of their devotion. They hold angels—the perfect servants of good—as their ideals, and incorporate images of angelic wings into their helmets or coats of arms.
  3868|
  3869|#### Tenets of Devotion
  3870|
  3871|Though the exact words and strictures of the Oath of Devotion vary, paladins of this oath share these tenets.
  3872|
  3873|Honesty. Don't lie or cheat. Let your word be your promise.
  3874|
  3875|Courage. Never fear to act, though caution is wise.
  3876|
  3877|Compassion. Aid others, protect the weak, and punish those who threaten them. Show mercy to your foes, but temper it with wisdom.
  3878|
  3879|Honor. Treat others with fairness, and let your honorable deeds be an example to them. Do as much good as possible while causing the least amount of harm.
  3880|
  3881|Duty. Be responsible for your actions and their consequences, protect those entrusted to your care, and obey those who have just authority over you.
  3882|
  3883|#### Oath Spells
  3884|
  3885|You gain oath spells at the paladin levels listed.
  3886|
  3887|
  3888|Oath of Devotion Spells
  3889|
  3890|
  3891|
  3892|
  3893|
  3894|
  3895|Paladin Level
  3896|Spells
  3897|
  3898|
  3899|
  3900|
  3901|3rd
  3902|protection from evil and good, sanctuary
  3903|
  3904|
  3905|5th
  3906|lesser restoration, zone of truth
  3907|
  3908|
  3909|9th
  3910|beacon of hope, dispel magic
  3911|
  3912|
  3913|13th
  3914|freedom of movement, guardian of faith
  3915|
  3916|
  3917|17th
  3918|commune, flame strike
  3919|
  3920|
  3921|
  3922|
  3923|#### Channel Divinity
  3924|
  3925|When you take this oath at 3rd level, you gain the following two Channel Divinity options.
  3926|
  3927|Sacred Weapon. As an action, you can imbue one weapon that you are holding with positive energy, using your Channel Divinity. For 1 minute, you add your Charisma modifier to attack rolls made with that weapon (with a minimum bonus of +1). The weapon also emits bright light in a 20—foot radius and dim light 20 feet beyond that. If the weapon is not already magical, it becomes magical for the duration.
  3928|
  3929|You can end this effect on your turn as part of any other action. If you are no longer holding or carrying this weapon, or if you fall unconscious, this effect ends.
  3930|
  3931|Turn the Unholy. As an action, you present your holy symbol and speak a prayer censuring fiends and undead, using your Channel Divinity. Each fiend or undead that can see or hear you within 30 feet of you must make a Wisdom saving throw. If the creature fails its saving throw, it is turned for 1 minute or until it takes damage.
  3932|
  3933|A turned creature must spend its turns trying to move as far away from you as it can, and it can't willingly move to a space within 30 feet of you. It also can't take reactions. For its action, it can use only the Dash action or try to escape from an effect that prevents it from moving. If there's nowhere to move, the creature can use the Dodge action.
  3934|
  3935|#### Aura of Devotion
  3936|
  3937|Starting at 7th level, you and friendly creatures within 10 feet of you can't be charmed while you are conscious.
  3938|
  3939|At 18th level, the range of this aura increases to 30 feet.
  3940|
  3941|#### Purity of Spirit
  3942|
  3943|Beginning at 15th level, you are always under the effects of a protection from evil and good spell.
  3944|
  3945|#### Holy Nimbus
  3946|
  3947|At 20th level, as an action, you can emanate an aura of sunlight. For 1 minute, bright light shines from you in a 30—foot radius, and dim light shines 30 feet beyond that.
  3948|
  3949|Whenever an enemy creature starts its turn in the bright light, the creature takes 10 radiant damage.
  3950|
  3951|In addition, for the duration, you have advantage on saving throws against spells cast by fiends or undead.
  3952|
  3953|Once you use this feature, you can't use it again until you finish a long rest.
  3954|

  4096|
  4097|
  4098|
  4099|As a ranger, you gain the following class features.
  4100|
  4101|#### Hit Points
  4102|
  4103|Hit Dice: 1d10 per ranger level
  4104|
  4105|Hit Points at 1st Level: 10 + your Constitution modifier
  4106|
  4107|Hit Points at Higher Levels: 1d10 (or 6) + your Constitution modifier per ranger level after 1st
  4108|
  4109|#### Proficiencies
  4110|
  4111|Armor: Light armor, medium armor, shields
  4112|
  4113|Weapons: Simple weapons, martial weapons
  4114|
  4115|Tools: None
  4116|
  4117|Saving Throws: Strength, Dexterity
  4118|
  4119|Skills: Choose three from Animal Handling, Athletics, Insight, Investigation, Nature, Perception, Stealth, and Survival
  4120|
  4121|#### Equipment
  4122|
  4123|You start with the following equipment, in addition to the equipment granted by your background:
  4124|
  4125|-   (a) scale mail or (b) leather armor
  4126|-   (a) two shortswords or (b) two simple melee weapons
  4127|-   (a) a dungeoneer's pack or (b) an explorer's pack
  4128|-   A longbow and a quiver of 20 arrows
  4129|
  4130|### Favored Enemy
  4131|
  4132|Beginning at 1st level, you have significant experience studying, tracking, hunting, and even talking to a certain type of enemy.
  4133|
  4134|Choose a type of favored enemy: aberrations, beasts, celestials, constructs, dragons, elementals, fey, fiends, giants, monstrosities, oozes, plants, or undead. Alternatively, you can select two races of humanoid (such as gnolls and orcs) as favored enemies.
  4135|
  4136|You have advantage on Wisdom (Survival) checks to track your favored enemies, as well as on Intelligence checks to recall information about them.
  4137|
  4138|When you gain this feature, you also learn one language of your choice that is spoken by your favored enemies, if they speak one at all.
  4139|
  4140|You choose one additional favored enemy, as well as an associated language, at 6th and 14th level. As you gain levels, your choices should reflect the types of monsters you have encountered on your adventures.
  4141|
  4142|### Natural Explorer
  4143|
  4144|You are particularly familiar with one type of natural environment and are adept at traveling and surviving in such regions. Choose one type of favored terrain: arctic, coast, desert, forest, grassland, mountain, or swamp. When you make an Intelligence or Wisdom check related to your favored terrain, your proficiency bonus is doubled if you are using a skill that you're proficient in.
  4145|
  4146|While traveling for an hour or more in your favored terrain, you gain the following benefits:
  4147|
  4148|-   Difficult terrain doesn't slow your group's travel.
  4149|-   Your group can't become lost except by magical means.
  4150|-   Even when you are engaged in another activity while traveling (such as foraging, navigating, or tracking), you remain alert to danger.
  4151|-   If you are traveling alone, you can move stealthily at a normal pace.
  4152|-   When you forage, you find twice as much food as you normally would.
  4153|-   While tracking other creatures, you also learn their exact number, their sizes, and how long ago they passed through the area.
  4154|
  4155|You choose additional favored terrain types at 6th and 10th level.
  4156|
  4157|### Fighting Style
  4158|
  4159|At 2nd level, you adopt a particular style of fighting as your specialty. Choose one of the following options. You can't take a Fighting Style option more than once, even if you later get to choose again.
  4160|
  4161|#### Archery
  4162|
  4163|You gain a +2 bonus to attack rolls you make with ranged weapons.
  4164|
  4165|#### Defense
  4166|
  4167|While you are wearing armor, you gain a +1 bonus to AC.
  4168|
  4169|#### Dueling
  4170|
  4171|When you are wielding a melee weapon in one hand and no other weapons, you gain a +2 bonus to damage rolls with that weapon.
  4172|
  4173|#### Two-Weapon Fighting
  4174|
  4175|When you engage in two-weapon fighting, you can add your ability modifier to the damage of the second attack.
  4176|
  4177|### Spellcasting
  4178|
  4179|By the time you reach 2nd level, you have learned to use the magical essence of nature to cast spells, much as a druid does. See "Spellcasting" and the ranger spell list.
  4180|
  4181|#### Spell Slots
  4182|
  4183|

  4280|10th
  4281|4
  4282|3
  4283|2
  4284|—
  4285|—
  4286|
  4287|
  4288|11th
  4289|4
  4290|3
  4291|3
  4292|—
  4293|—
  4294|
  4295|
  4296|12th
  4297|4
  4298|3
  4299|3
  4300|—
  4301|—

  4336|17th
  4337|4
  4338|3
  4339|3
  4340|3
  4341|1
  4342|
  4343|
  4344|18th
  4345|4
  4346|3
  4347|3
  4348|3
  4349|1
  4350|
  4351|
  4352|19th
  4353|4
  4354|3
  4355|3
  4356|3
  4357|2
  4358|
  4359|
  4360|20th
  4361|4
  4362|3
  4363|3
  4364|3
  4365|2
  4366|
  4367|
  4368|
  4369|
  4370|The Ranger Spell Slots by Level table shows how many spell slots you have to cast your spells of 1st level and higher. To cast one of these spells, you must expend a slot of the spell's level or higher. You regain all expended spell slots when you finish a long rest.
  4371|
  4372|For example, if you know the 1st-level spell speak with animals and have a 1st-level and a 2nd-level spell slot available, you can cast speak with animals using either slot.
  4373|
  4374|#### Spells Known of 1st Level and Higher
  4375|
  4376|You know two 1st-level spells of your choice from the ranger spell list.
  4377|
  4378|The Spells Known column of the Ranger table shows when you learn more ranger spells of your choice. Each of these spells must be of a level for which you have spell slots. For instance, when you reach 5th level in this class, you can learn one new spell of 1st or 2nd level.
  4379|
  4380|Additionally, when you gain a level in this class, you can choose one of the ranger spells you know and replace it with another spell from the ranger spell list, which also must be of a level for which you have spell slots.
  4381|
  4382|#### Spellcasting Ability
  4383|
  4384|Wisdom is your spellcasting ability for your ranger spells, since your magic draws on your attunement to nature. You use your Wisdom whenever a spell refers to your spellcasting ability. In addition, you use your Wisdom modifier when setting the saving throw DC for a ranger spell you cast and when making an attack roll with one.
  4385|
  4386|
  4387|
  4388|
  4389|
  4390|
  4391|
  4392|Spell save DC = 8 + your proficiency bonus + your Wisdom modifier
  4393|
  4394|
  4395|Spell attack modifier = your proficiency bonus + your Wisdom modifier
  4396|
  4397|
  4398|
  4399|
  4400|### Ranger Archetype
  4401|
  4402|At 3rd level, you choose an archetype (see "Ranger Archetypes") that you strive to emulate. Your choice grants you features at 3rd level and again at 7th, 11th, and 15th level.
  4403|
  4404|### Primeval Awareness
  4405|
  4406|Beginning at 3rd level, you can use your action and expend one ranger spell slot to focus your awareness on the region around you. For 1 minute per level of the spell slot you expend, you can sense whether the following types of creatures are present within 1 mile of you (or within up to 6 miles if you are in your favored terrain): aberrations, celestials, dragons, elementals, fey, fiends, and undead. This feature doesn't reveal the creatures' location or number.
  4407|
  4408|### Ability Score Improvement
  4409|
  4410|When you reach 4th level, and again at 8th, 12th, 16th, and 19th level, you can increase one ability score of your choice by 2, or you can increase two ability scores of your choice by 1. As normal, you can't increase an ability score above 20 using this feature.
  4411|
  4412|### Extra Attack
  4413|
  4414|Beginning at 5th level, you can attack twice, instead of once, whenever you take the Attack action on your turn.
  4415|
  4416|### Land's Stride
  4417|
  4418|Starting at 8th level, moving through nonmagical difficult terrain costs you no extra movement. You can also pass through nonmagical plants without being slowed by them and without taking damage from them if they have thorns, spines, or a similar hazard.
  4419|
  4420|In addition, you have advantage on saving throws against plants that are magically created or manipulated to impede movement, such those created by the entangle spell.
  4421|
  4422|### Hide in Plain Sight
  4423|
  4424|Starting at 10th level, you can spend 1 minute creating camouflage for yourself. You must have access to fresh mud, dirt, plants, soot, and other naturally occurring materials with which to create your camouflage.
  4425|
  4426|Once you are camouflaged in this way, you can try to hide by pressing yourself up against a solid surface, such as a tree or wall, that is at least as tall and wide as you are. You gain a +10 bonus to Dexterity (Stealth) checks as long as you remain there without moving or taking actions. Once you move or take an action or a reaction, you must camouflage yourself again to gain this benefit.
  4427|
  4428|### Vanish
  4429|
  4430|Starting at 14th level, you can use the Hide action as a bonus action on your turn. Also, you can't be tracked by nonmagical means, unless you choose to leave a trail.
  4431|
  4432|### Feral Senses
  4433|
  4434|At 18th level, you gain preternatural senses that help you fight creatures you can't see. When you attack a creature you can't see, your inability to see it doesn't impose disadvantage on your attack rolls against it. You are also aware of the location of any invisible creature within 30 feet of you, provided that the creature isn't hidden from you and you aren't blinded or deafened.
  4435|
  4436|### Foe Slayer
  4437|
  4438|At 20th level, you become an unparalleled hunter of your enemies. Once on each of your turns, you can add your Wisdom modifier to the attack roll or the damage roll of an attack you make against one of your favored enemies. You can choose to use this feature before or after the roll, but before any effects of the roll are applied.
  4439|
  4440|## Ranger Archetypes {#section-ranger-archetypes}
  4441|
  4442|A classic expression of the ranger ideal is the Hunter.
  4443|
  4444|### Hunter
  4445|
  4446|Emulating the Hunter archetype means accepting your place as a bulwark between civilization and the terrors of the wilderness. As you walk the Hunter's path, you learn specialized techniques for fighting the threats you face, from rampaging ogres and hordes of orcs to towering giants and terrifying dragons.
  4447|
  4448|#### Hunter's Prey
  4449|
  4450|At 3rd level, you gain one of the following features of your choice.
  4451|
  4452|Colossus Slayer. Your tenacity can wear down the most potent foes. When you hit a creature with a weapon attack, the creature takes an extra 1d8 damage if it's below its hit point maximum. You can deal this extra damage only once per turn.
  4453|
  4454|Giant Killer. When a Large or larger creature within 5 feet of you hits or misses you with an attack, you can use your reaction to attack that creature immediately after its attack, provided that you can see the creature.
  4455|
  4456|Horde Breaker. Once on each of your turns when you make a weapon attack, you can make another attack with the same weapon against a different creature that is within 5 feet of the original target and within range of your weapon.
  4457|
  4458|#### Defensive Tactics
  4459|
  4460|At 7th level, you gain one of the following features of your choice.
  4461|
  4462|Escape the Horde. Opportunity attacks against you are made with disadvantage.
  4463|
  4464|Multiattack Defense. When a creature hits you with an attack, you gain a +4 bonus to AC against all subsequent attacks made by that creature for the rest of the turn.
  4465|
  4466|Steel Will. You have advantage on saving throws against being frightened.
  4467|
  4468|#### Multiattack
  4469|
  4470|At 11th level, you gain one of the following features of your choice.
  4471|
  4472|Volley. You can use your action to make a ranged attack against any number of creatures within 10 feet of a point you can see within your weapon's range. You must have ammunition for each target, as normal, and you make a separate attack roll for each target.
  4473|
  4474|Whirlwind Attack. You can use your action to make a melee attack against any number of creatures within 5 feet of you, with a separate attack roll for each target.
  4475|
  4476|#### Superior Hunter's Defense
  4477|
  4478|At 15th level, you gain one of the following features of your choice.
  4479|
  4480|Evasion. When you are subjected to an effect, such as a red dragon's fiery breath or a lightning bolt spell, that allows you to make a Dexterity saving throw to take only half damage, you instead take no damage if you succeed on the saving throw, and only half damage if you fail.
  4481|
  4482|Stand Against the Tide. When a hostile creature misses you with a melee attack, you can use your reaction to force that creature to repeat the same attack against another creature (other than itself) of your choice.
  4483|
  4484|Uncanny Dodge. When an attacker that you can see hits you with an attack, you can use your reaction to halve the attack's damage against you.
  4485|
  4486|## Rogue {#section-rogue}
  4487|
  4488|### Class Features
  4489|
  4490|
  4491|The Rogue
  4492|
  4493|
  4494|
  4495|
  4496|
  4497|
  4498|
  4499|
  4500|Level
  4501|Proficiency Bonus
  4502|Sneak Attack
  4503|Features
  4504|
  4505|
  4506|
  4507|
  4508|1st
  4509|+2
  4510|1d6
  4511|Expertise, Sneak Attack, Thieves' Cant
  4512|
  4513|
  4514|2nd
  4515|+2

  4517|Cunning Action
  4518|
  4519|
  4520|3rd

  4553|Ability Score Improvement
  4554|
  4555|
  4556|9th
  4557|+4
  4558|5d6
  4559|Roguish Archetype feature
  4560|
  4561|
  4562|10th
  4563|+4
  4564|5d6
  4565|Ability Score Improvement
  4566|
  4567|
  4568|11th
  4569|+4

  4571|Reliable Talent
  4572|
  4573|
  4574|12th
  4575|+4
  4576|6d6
  4577|Ability Score Improvement
  4578|
  4579|
  4580|13th
  4581|+5
  4582|7d6
  4583|Roguish Archetype feature
  4584|
  4585|
  4586|14th
  4587|+5
  4588|7d6
  4589|Blindsense
  4590|
  4591|
  4592|15th
  4593|+5
  4594|8d6
  4595|Slippery Mind
  4596|
  4597|
  4598|16th
  4599|+5
  4600|8d6
  4601|Ability Score Improvement
  4602|
  4603|
  4604|17th
  4605|+6
  4606|9d6
  4607|Roguish Archetype feature
  4608|
  4609|
  4610|18th
  4611|+6
  4612|9d6
  4613|Elusive
  4614|
  4615|
  4616|19th
  4617|+6
  4618|10d6
  4619|Ability Score Improvement
  4620|
  4621|
  4622|20th
  4623|+6
  4624|10d6
  4625|Stroke of Luck
  4626|
  4627|
  4628|
  4629|
  4630|As a rogue, you have the following class features.
  4631|
  4632|#### Hit Points
  4633|
  4634|Hit Dice: 1d8 per rogue level
  4635|
  4636|Hit Points at 1st Level: 8 + your Constitution modifier
  4637|
  4638|Hit Points at Higher Levels: 1d8 (or 5) + your Constitution modifier per rogue level after 1st
  4639|
  4640|#### Proficiencies
  4641|
  4642|Armor: Light armor
  4643|
  4644|Weapons: Simple weapons, hand crossbows, longswords, rapiers, shortswords
  4645|
  4646|Tools: Thieves' tools
  4647|
  4648|Saving Throws: Dexterity, Intelligence
  4649|
  4650|Skills: Choose four from Acrobatics, Athletics, Deception, Insight, Intimidation, Investigation, Perception, Performance, Persuasion, Sleight of Hand, and Stealth
  4651|
  4652|#### Equipment
  4653|
  4654|You start with the following equipment, in addition to the equipment granted by your background:
  4655|
  4656|-   (a) a rapier or (b) a shortsword
  4657|-   (a) a shortbow and quiver of 20 arrows or (b) a shortsword
  4658|-   (a) a burglar's pack, (b) a dungeoneer's pack, or (c) an explorer's pack
  4659|-   (a) Leather armor, two daggers, and thieves' tools
  4660|
  4661|### Expertise
  4662|
  4663|At 1st level, choose two of your skill proficiencies, or one of your skill proficiencies and your proficiency with thieves' tools. Your proficiency bonus is doubled for any ability check you make that uses either of the chosen proficiencies.
  4664|
  4665|At 6th level, you can choose two more of your proficiencies (in skills or with thieves' tools) to gain this benefit.
  4666|
  4667|### Sneak Attack
  4668|
  4669|Beginning at 1st level, you know how to strike subtly and exploit a foe's distraction. Once per turn, you can deal an extra 1d6 damage to one creature you hit with an attack if you have advantage on the attack roll. The attack must use a finesse or a ranged weapon.
  4670|
  4671|You don't need advantage on the attack roll if another enemy of the target is within 5 feet of it, that enemy isn't incapacitated, and you don't have disadvantage on the attack roll.
  4672|
  4673|The amount of the extra damage increases as you gain levels in this class, as shown in the Sneak Attack column of the Rogue table.
  4674|
  4675|### Thieves' Cant
  4676|
  4677|During your rogue training you learned thieves' cant, a secret mix of dialect, jargon, and code that allows you to hide messages in seemingly normal conversation. Only another creature that knows thieves' cant understands such messages. It takes four times longer to convey such a message than it does to speak the same idea plainly.
  4678|
  4679|In addition, you understand a set of secret signs and symbols used to convey short, simple messages, such as whether an area is dangerous or the territory of a thieves' guild, whether loot is nearby, or whether the people in an area are easy marks or will provide a safe house for thieves on the run.
  4680|
  4681|### Cunning Action
  4682|
  4683|Starting at 2nd level, your quick thinking and agility allow you to move and act quickly. You can take a bonus action on each of your turns in combat. This action can be used only to take the Dash, Disengage, or Hide action.
  4684|
  4685|### Roguish Archetype
  4686|
  4687|At 3rd level, you choose an archetype (see "Roguish Archetypes") that you emulate in the exercise of your rogue abilities. Your archetype choice grants you features at 3rd level and then again at 9th, 13th, and 17th level.
  4688|
  4689|### Ability Score Improvement
  4690|
  4691|When you reach 4th level, and again at 8th, 10th, 12th, 16th, and 19th level, you can increase one ability score of your choice by 2, or you can increase two ability scores of your choice by 1. As normal, you can't increase an ability score above 20 using this feature.
  4692|
  4693|### Uncanny Dodge
  4694|
  4695|Starting at 5th level, when an attacker that you can see hits you with an attack, you can use your reaction to halve the attack's damage against you.
  4696|
  4697|### Evasion
  4698|
  4699|Beginning at 7th level, you can nimbly dodge out of the way of certain area effects, such as a red dragon's fiery breath or an ice storm spell. When you are subjected to an effect that allows you to make a Dexterity saving throw to take only half damage, you instead take no damage if you succeed on the saving throw, and only half damage if you fail.
  4700|
  4701|### Reliable Talent
  4702|
  4703|By 11th level, you have refined your chosen skills until they approach perfection. Whenever you make an ability check that lets you add your proficiency bonus, you can treat a d20 roll of 9 or lower as a 10.
  4704|
  4705|### Blindsense
  4706|
  4707|Starting at 14th level, if you are able to hear, you are aware of the location of any hidden or invisible creature within 10 feet of you.
  4708|
  4709|### Slippery Mind
  4710|
  4711|By 15th level, you have acquired greater mental strength. You gain proficiency in Wisdom saving throws.
  4712|
  4713|### Elusive
  4714|
  4715|Beginning at 18th level, you are so evasive that attackers rarely gain the upper hand against you. No attack roll has advantage against you while you aren't incapacitated.
  4716|
  4717|### Stroke of Luck
  4718|
  4719|At 20th level, you have an uncanny knack for succeeding when you need to. If your attack misses a target within range, you can turn the miss into a hit. Alternatively, if you fail an ability check, you can treat the d20 roll as a 20.
  4720|
  4721|Once you use this feature, you can't use it again until you finish a short or long rest.
  4722|
  4723|## Roguish Archetypes {#section-roguish-archetypes}
  4724|
  4725|Rogues have many features in common, including their emphasis on perfecting their skills, their precise and deadly approach to combat, and their increasingly quick reflexes. But different rogues steer those talents in varying directions, embodied by the rogue archetypes. Your choice of archetype is a reflection of your focus—not necessarily an indication of your chosen profession, but a description of your preferred techniques.
  4726|
  4727|### Thief
  4728|
  4729|You hone your skills in the larcenous arts. Burglars, bandits, cutpurses, and other criminals typically follow this archetype, but so do rogues who prefer to think of themselves as professional treasure seekers, explorers, delvers, and investigators. In addition to improving your agility and stealth, you learn skills useful for delving into ancient ruins, reading unfamiliar languages, and using magic items you normally couldn't employ.
  4730|
  4731|#### Fast Hands
  4732|
  4733|Starting at 3rd level, you can use the bonus action granted by your Cunning Action to make a Dexterity (Sleight of Hand) check, use your thieves' tools to disarm a trap or open a lock, or take the Use an Object action.
  4734|
  4735|#### Second-Story Work
  4736|
  4737|When you choose this archetype at 3rd level, you gain the ability to climb faster than normal; climbing no longer costs you extra movement.
  4738|
  4739|In addition, when you make a running jump, the distance you cover increases by a number of feet equal to your Dexterity modifier.
  4740|
  4741|#### Supreme Sneak
  4742|
  4743|Starting at 9th level, you have advantage on a Dexterity (Stealth) check if you move no more than half your speed on the same turn.
  4744|
  4745|#### Use Magic Device
  4746|
  4747|By 13th level, you have learned enough about the workings of magic that you can improvise the use of items even when they are not intended for you. You ignore all class, race, and level requirements on the use of magic items.
  4748|
  4749|#### Thief's Reflexes
  4750|
  4751|When you reach 17th level, you have become adept at laying ambushes and quickly escaping danger. You can take two turns during the first round of any combat. You take your first turn at your normal initiative and your second turn at your initiative minus 10. You can't use this feature when you are surprised.
  4752|
  4753|## Sorcerer {#section-sorcerer}

  4934|Sorcerous Restoration
  4935|6

  4938|
  4939|
  4940|
  4941|As a sorcerer, you gain the following class features.
  4942|
  4943|#### Hit Points
  4944|
  4945|Hit Dice: 1d6 per sorcerer level
  4946|
  4947|Hit Points at 1st Level: 6 + your Constitution modifier
  4948|
  4949|Hit Points at Higher Levels: 1d6 (or 4) + your Constitution modifier per sorcerer level after 1st
  4950|
  4951|#### Proficiencies
  4952|
  4953|Armor: None
  4954|
  4955|Weapons: Daggers, darts, slings, quarterstaffs, light crossbows
  4956|
  4957|Tools: None
  4958|
  4959|Saving Throws: Constitution, Charisma
  4960|
  4961|Skills: Choose two from Arcana, Deception, Insight, Intimidation, Persuasion, and Religion
  4962|
  4963|#### Equipment
  4964|
  4965|You start with the following equipment, in addition to the equipment granted by your background:
  4966|
  4967|-   (a) a light crossbow and 20 bolts or (b) any simple weapon
  4968|-   (a) a component pouch or (b) an arcane focus
  4969|-   (a) a dungeoneer's pack or (b) an explorer's pack
  4970|-   Two daggers
  4971|
  4972|### Spellcasting
  4973|
  4974|An event in your past, or in the life of a parent or ancestor, left an indelible mark on you, infusing you with arcane magic. This font of magic, whatever its origin, fuels your spells.
  4975|
  4976|#### Cantrips
  4977|
  4978|At 1st level, you know four cantrips of your choice from the sorcerer spell list. You learn additional sorcerer cantrips of your choice at higher levels, as shown in the Cantrips Known column of the Sorcerer table.
  4979|
  4980|#### Spell Slots
  4981|
  4982|

  5243|20th
  5244|4
  5245|3
  5246|3
  5247|3
  5248|3
  5249|2
  5250|2
  5251|1
  5252|1
  5253|
  5254|
  5255|
  5256|
  5257|The Sorcerer Spell Slots per Level table shows how many spell slots you have to cast your spells of 1st level and higher. To cast one of these sorcerer spells, you must expend a slot of the spell's level or higher. You regain all expended spell slots when you finish a long rest.
  5258|
  5259|For example, if you know the 1st-level spell burning hands and have a 1st-level and a 2nd-level spell slot available, you can cast burning hands using either slot.
  5260|
  5261|#### Spells Known of 1st Level and Higher
  5262|
  5263|You know two 1st-level spells of your choice from the sorcerer spell list.
  5264|
  5265|The Spells Known column of the Sorcerer table shows when you learn more sorcerer spells of your choice. Each of these spells must be of a level for which you have spell slots. For instance, when you reach 3rd level in this class, you can learn one new spell of 1st or 2nd level.
  5266|
  5267|Additionally, when you gain a level in this class, you can choose one of the sorcerer spells you know and replace it with another spell from the sorcerer spell list, which also must be of a level for which you have spell slots.
  5268|
  5269|#### Spellcasting Ability
  5270|
  5271|Charisma is your spellcasting ability for your sorcerer spells, since the power of your magic relies on your ability to project your will into the world. You use your Charisma whenever a spell refers to your spellcasting ability. In addition, you use your Charisma modifier when setting the saving throw DC for a sorcerer spell you cast and when making an attack roll with one.
  5272|
  5273|
  5274|
  5275|
  5276|
  5277|
  5278|
  5279|Spell save DC = 8 + your proficiency bonus + your Charisma modifier
  5280|
  5281|
  5282|Spell attack modifier = your proficiency bonus + your Charisma modifier
  5283|
  5284|
  5285|
  5286|
  5287|#### Spellcasting Focus
  5288|
  5289|You can use an arcane focus as a spellcasting focus for your sorcerer spells.
  5290|
  5291|### Sorcerous Origin
  5292|
  5293|Choose a sorcerous origin (see "Sorcerous Origins"), which describes the source of your innate magical power.
  5294|
  5295|Your choice grants you features when you choose it at 1st level and again at 6th, 14th, and 18th level.
  5296|
  5297|### Font of Magic
  5298|
  5299|At 2nd level, you tap into a deep wellspring of magic within yourself. This wellspring is represented by sorcery points, which allow you to create a variety of magical effects.
  5300|
  5301|#### Sorcery Points
  5302|
  5303|You have 2 sorcery points, and you gain more as you reach higher levels, as shown in the Sorcery Points column of the Sorcerer table. You can never have more sorcery points than shown on the table for your level. You regain all spent sorcery points when you finish a long rest.
  5304|
  5305|#### Flexible Casting
  5306|
  5307|You can use your sorcery points to gain additional spell slots, or sacrifice spell slots to gain additional sorcery points. You learn other ways to use your sorcery points as you reach higher levels.
  5308|
  5309|Creating Spell Slots. You can transform unexpended sorcery points into one spell slot as a bonus action on your turn. The Creating Spell Slots table shows the cost of creating a spell slot of a given level. You can create spell slots no higher in level than 5th.
  5310|
  5311|Any spell slot you create with this feature vanishes when you finish a long rest.
  5312|
  5313|
  5314|Creating Spell Slots
  5315|
  5316|
  5317|
  5318|
  5319|
  5320|
  5321|Spell Slot Level
  5322|Sorcery Point Cost
  5323|
  5324|
  5325|
  5326|
  5327|1st
  5328|2
  5329|
  5330|
  5331|2nd
  5332|3
  5333|
  5334|
  5335|3rd
  5336|5
  5337|
  5338|
  5339|4th
  5340|6
  5341|
  5342|
  5343|5th
  5344|7
  5345|
  5346|
  5347|
  5348|
  5349|Converting a Spell Slot to Sorcery Points. As a bonus action on your turn, you can expend one spell slot and gain a number of sorcery points equal to the slot's level.
  5350|
  5351|### Metamagic
  5352|
  5353|At 3rd level, you gain the ability to twist your spells to suit your needs. You gain two of the following Metamagic options of your choice. You gain another one at 10th and 17th level.
  5354|
  5355|You can use only one Metamagic option on a spell when you cast it, unless otherwise noted.
  5356|
  5357|#### Careful Spell
  5358|
  5359|When you cast a spell that forces other creatures to make a saving throw, you can protect some of those creatures from the spell's full force. To do so, you spend 1 sorcery point and choose a number of those creatures up to your Charisma modifier (minimum of one creature). A chosen creature automatically succeeds on its saving throw against the spell.
  5360|
  5361|#### Distant Spell
  5362|
  5363|When you cast a spell that has a range of 5 feet or greater, you can spend 1 sorcery point to double the range of the spell.
  5364|
  5365|When you cast a spell that has a range of touch, you can spend 1 sorcery point to make the range of the spell 30 feet.
  5366|
  5367|#### Empowered Spell
  5368|
  5369|When you roll damage for a spell, you can spend 1 sorcery point to reroll a number of the damage dice up to your Charisma modifier (minimum of one). You must use the new rolls.
  5370|
  5371|You can use Empowered Spell even if you have already used a different Metamagic option during the casting of the spell.
  5372|
  5373|#### Extended Spell
  5374|
  5375|When you cast a spell that has a duration of 1 minute or longer, you can spend 1 sorcery point to double its duration, to a maximum duration of 24 hours.
  5376|
  5377|#### Heightened Spell
  5378|
  5379|When you cast a spell that forces a creature to make a saving throw to resist its effects, you can spend 3 sorcery points to give one target of the spell disadvantage on its first saving throw made against the spell.
  5380|
  5381|#### Quickened Spell
  5382|
  5383|When you cast a spell that has a casting time of 1 action, you can spend 2 sorcery points to change the casting time to 1 bonus action for this casting.
  5384|
  5385|#### Subtle Spell
  5386|
  5387|When you cast a spell, you can spend 1 sorcery point to cast it without any somatic or verbal components.
  5388|
  5389|#### Twinned Spell
  5390|
  5391|When you cast a spell that targets only one creature and doesn't have a range of self, you can spend a number of sorcery points equal to the spell's level to target a second creature in range with the same spell (1 sorcery point if the spell is a cantrip).
  5392|
  5393|To be eligible, a spell must be incapable of targeting more than one creature at the spell's current level. For example, magic missile and scorching ray aren't eligible, but ray of frost and finger of death are.
  5394|
  5395|### Ability Score Improvement
  5396|
  5397|When you reach 4th level, and again at 8th, 12th, 16th, and 19th level, you can increase one ability score of your choice by 2, or you can increase two ability scores of your choice by 1. As normal, you can't increase an ability score above 20 using this feature.
  5398|
  5399|### Sorcerous Restoration
  5400|
  5401|At 20th level, you regain 4 expended sorcery points whenever you finish a short rest.
  5402|
  5403|## Sorcerous Origins {#section-sorcerous-origins}
  5404|
  5405|Different sorcerers claim different origins for their innate magic.
  5406|
  5407|### Draconic Bloodline
  5408|
  5409|Your innate magic comes from draconic magic that was mingled with your blood or that of your ancestors. Most often, sorcerers with this origin trace their descent back to a mighty sorcerer of ancient times who made a bargain with a dragon or who might even have claimed a dragon parent. Some of these bloodlines are well established in the world, but most are obscure. Any given sorcerer could be the first of a new bloodline, as a result of a pact or some other exceptional circumstance.
  5410|
  5411|#### Dragon Ancestor
  5412|
  5413|At 1st level, you choose one type of dragon as your ancestor. The damage type associated with each dragon is used by features you gain later.
  5414|
  5415|
  5416|Draconic Ancestry
  5417|
  5418|
  5419|
  5420|
  5421|
  5422|
  5423|Dragon
  5424|Damage Type
  5425|
  5426|
  5427|
  5428|
  5429|Black
  5430|Acid
  5431|
  5432|
  5433|Blue
  5434|Lightning
  5435|
  5436|
  5437|Brass
  5438|Fire
  5439|
  5440|
  5441|Bronze
  5442|Lightning
  5443|
  5444|
  5445|Copper
  5446|Acid
  5447|
  5448|
  5449|Gold
  5450|Fire
  5451|
  5452|
  5453|Green
  5454|Poison
  5455|
  5456|
  5457|Red
  5458|Fire
  5459|
  5460|
  5461|Silver
  5462|Cold
  5463|
  5464|
  5465|White
  5466|Cold
  5467|
  5468|
  5469|
  5470|
  5471|You can speak, read, and write Draconic. Additionally, whenever you make a Charisma check when interacting with dragons, your proficiency bonus is doubled if it applies to the check.
  5472|
  5473|#### Draconic Resilience
  5474|
  5475|As magic flows through your body, it causes physical traits of your dragon ancestors to emerge. At 1st level, your hit point maximum increases by 1 and increases by 1 again whenever you gain a level in this class.
  5476|
  5477|Additionally, parts of your skin are covered by a thin sheen of dragon-like scales. When you aren't wearing armor, your AC equals 13 + your Dexterity modifier.
  5478|
  5479|#### Elemental Affinity
  5480|
  5481|Starting at 6th level, when you cast a spell that deals damage of the type associated with your draconic ancestry, you can add your Charisma modifier to one damage roll of that spell. At the same time, you can spend 1 sorcery point to gain resistance to that damage type for 1 hour.
  5482|
  5483|#### Dragon Wings
  5484|
  5485|At 14th level, you gain the ability to sprout a pair of dragon wings from your back, gaining a flying speed equal to your current speed. You can create these wings as a bonus action on your turn. They last until you dismiss them as a bonus action on your turn.
  5486|
  5487|You can't manifest your wings while wearing armor unless the armor is made to accommodate them, and clothing not made to accommodate your wings might be destroyed when you manifest them.
  5488|
  5489|#### Draconic Presence
  5490|
  5491|Beginning at 18th level, you can channel the dread presence of your dragon ancestor, causing those around you to become awestruck or frightened. As an action, you can spend 5 sorcery points to draw on this power and exude an aura of awe or fear (your choice) to a distance of 60 feet. For 1 minute or until you lose your concentration (as if you were casting a concentration spell), each hostile creature that starts its turn in this aura must succeed on a Wisdom saving throw or be charmed (if you chose awe) or frightened (if you chose fear) until the aura ends. A creature that succeeds on this saving throw is immune to your aura for 24 hours.
  5492|

  5671|20th
  5672|+6
  5673|Eldritch Master
  5674|4
  5675|15
  5676|8
  5677|
  5678|
  5679|
  5680|
  5681|As a warlock, you gain the following class features.
  5682|
  5683|#### Hit Points
  5684|
  5685|Hit Dice: 1d8 per warlock level
  5686|
  5687|Hit Points at 1st Level: 8 + your Constitution modifier
  5688|
  5689|Hit Points at Higher Levels: 1d8 (or 5) + your Constitution modifier per warlock level after 1st
  5690|
  5691|#### Proficiencies
  5692|
  5693|Armor: Light armor
  5694|
  5695|Weapons: Simple weapons
  5696|
  5697|Tools: None
  5698|
  5699|Saving Throws: Wisdom, Charisma
  5700|
  5701|Skills: Choose two skills from Arcana, Deception, History, Intimidation, Investigation, Nature, and Religion
  5702|
  5703|#### Equipment
  5704|
  5705|You start with the following equipment, in addition to the equipment granted by your background:
  5706|
  5707|-   (a) a light crossbow and 20 bolts or (b) any simple weapon
  5708|-   (a) a component pouch or (b) an arcane focus
  5709|-   (a) a scholar's pack or (b) a dungeoneer's pack
  5710|-   Leather armor, any simple weapon, and two daggers
  5711|
  5712|### Otherworldly Patron
  5713|
  5714|At 1st level, you have struck a bargain with an otherworldly being of your choice, such as the Fiend. Your choice grants you features at 1st level and again at 6th, 10th, and 14th level.
  5715|
  5716|### Pact Magic
  5717|
  5718|Your arcane research and the magic bestowed on you by your patron have given you facility with spells.
  5719|
  5720|#### Cantrips
  5721|
  5722|You know two cantrips of your choice from the warlock spell list. You learn additional warlock cantrips of your choice at higher levels, as shown in the Cantrips Known column of the Warlock table.
  5723|
  5724|#### Spell Slots
  5725|
